base.counters: Swarming the Inferno Tower with numerous, low-Elixir units (like Goblins or Skeletons) is effective because it can only target one unit at a time. Alternatively, applying a 'reset' effect using stun spells (such as Zap or Electro Wizard) forces it to lose its charge, giving you an opportunity to destroy it. Strong continuous stunning effects prevent the damage ramp-up entirely.
base.combos: Pairing the Inferno Tower with stunnable cards (like Ice Spirit) is excellent; the stun keeps the enemy units pinned, allowing the Inferno Tower ample time to fully charge its devastating attack. Combining it with area damage or knockback effects (like Tornado or Wizard) helps protect the Inferno Tower from swarms while maximizing its ability to focus on a specific, high-value target.
tips: Due to its charge time, wait until the fourth second when using Lightning or Zap to reset its damage, maximizing your chances of dealing structural damage.
The Inferno Tower is vulnerable to Earthquake combined with Zap, which can severely cripple or completely take it out for less Elixir than multiple spells.
Position the Inferno Tower so that a critical high-value target (like the opponent's bridge) is within its optimal blast radius while limiting the chance of the opponent's spells hitting both towers.

base.how_it_works: The Knight is a single-target, ground-targeting troop that spawns with high hitpoints and moderate damage. It functions as a reliable melee fighter, allowing it to block damage from most single-target spells and troops.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use the Knight to tank enemy tower damage for other win condition units, like Goblin Barrel, forcing the opponent to commit defensive spells. Defensively, deploy him at the edge of your tower to soak up initial damage, thereby protecting other weaker units deployed further back. For situational use, if you anticipate the opponent having small spells like The Log or Barbarian Barrel, use him to engage the enemy early, forcing them to spend their primary counter.
base.counters: Area damage spells or splash units (like Wizard or Bomber) are highly effective because they can hit the Knight and anything behind him simultaneously. Swarms of low-HP units (such as Minions or Bats) can easily overwhelm him with minimal Elixir investment. High-damage single-target ranged units (like Mini P.E.K.K.A.) can quickly eliminate him before he can deal meaningful damage.
base.combos: Pairing the Knight with Goblin Barrel allows him to effectively tank tower hits, making the spawned Goblins harder to eliminate with small spells like Zap. Against building-targeting units, placing the Knight nearby lets him soak up hits while the unit deals damage to the tower, giving an early combat advantage. He also synergizes with Electro Wizard, whose stun can reset enemies to re-target the Knight.
base.tips: When countering Goblin Barrel, place the Knight on the side of the tower closest to the edge to ensure it targets the Goblins last, maximizing overall damage. Do not rely solely on the Knight to counter a Goblin Barrel; always supplement the defense with other card types like spells or area damage. When facing high-health splash troops, deploy the Knight at a considerable distance to ensure support units can be placed on top of him without being wiped out.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolved Knight gains a special Shield that significantly reduces the damage received when he is not attacking. He remains active after dealing damage, provided he is not hit by an opponent's attack.
evolution.evo_slot_value: This Evolution shines defensively by absorbing damage from ranged units, allowing support troops and towers to chip the enemy structure more efficiently. However, it may not be the optimal Evo choice if the current deck needs specialized area control or consistent offensive pressure that this shield mechanic cannot provide.
evolution.how_to_use: When playing defensively, it is preferred to position the Evolved Knight away from troops so he maximizes reduced damage until he engages the enemy unit.
evolution.counters: While ground melee troops may still be effective, the reduced damage shields make him more resistant to initial hits from ranged units and charges.
tips: Position him optimally to force range on the opponent's towers while minimizing damage to friendly support units.
Utilize his high health to protect low-health support troops from instant removal spells, keeping the push viable even after a spell is used.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Knight has the same general troop characteristics as the base card (high hitpoints and moderate damage), but unlike its Evolution, it no longer possesses the 65% damage reduction when not attacking.
hero.hero_ability: The Hero Knight has the 'Triumphant Taunt' ability, which costs 2 Elixir to activate. After a 1-second delay, it taunts every enemy troop and building in a 7.5-tiles range, forcing them to attack the Knight. The shield and taunt effect lasts for 5 seconds, and the ability has a 25-second cooldown after the duration ends.
hero.ability_timing: It is highly effective when activating during a coordinated attack, as it can force enemy troops to refocus on the Knight, allowing other units to deal sustained chip damage to the tower. Activating it defensively can pull enemy units away from the tower, or move the focus of opposing ranged or building units to another area of the map.
hero.hero_slot_value: It is valuable for controlling enemy attention and redirecting damage, which is crucial for facilitating massive pushes. However, slot usage must be considered against other high-utility units, as both Hero and Champion slots are limited and highly contested resources.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The core counter concepts remain the same, but the Knight’s lack of damage reduction when not attacking means it is less resilient to certain types of incoming damage compared to its Evolution.
tips: Using the Hero's ability defensively can pull multiple enemy troops, such as the two barrels of an Evolved Goblin Barrel, away from the main target for a potential negative Elixir trade.

By placing the Knight at the front line, he can absorb initial tower damage, and the ability can then be used when the remaining enemy forces are focused on the central towers, maximizing strategic impact.

base.how_it_works: The Lava Hound is a 7-Elixir air unit that targets buildings. When it is defeated, it spawns six spread-out Lava Pups, which are air troops that target anything. The Pups have lower hitpoints but deal slightly higher damage than the base Lava Hound.
base.how_to_use: Deploying the Lava Hound's main role is to act as a high-HP air tank, drawing enemy fire to protect lower-HP, high-damage air support units. It is best utilized when pairing it with units like Minion Horde or Electro Dragon. A crucial situational tip is to recognize that sending the Lava Hound alone is ineffective, as it lacks the staying power without support.
base.counters: Area damage spells like Arrows or Fireball are highly effective because they can instantly clear the six Lava Pups without needing precise targeting. The Inferno Tower is excellent as it deals high damage to the Lava Hound while the Hound deals minimal damage in return. For mixed counters, Minions can be sent to draw the enemy's focus and damage off the Inferno Tower, allowing the Lava Hound to continue attacking.
base.combos: Pairing the Lava Hound with Lightning is powerful because it eliminates common defensive structures and high-value units (like Wizards or Inferno Towers) that attempt to counter the tank. Another strong combo is using Tornado and the Executioner, which groups all opposing troops together, allowing the Executioner to hit the Hound and subsequently the spawned Lava Pups simultaneously.
tips: Be prepared to immediately counter the resulting Lava Pups with area damage, as they deal significant damage if left unchecked.
When using the Lava Hound, always consider the enemy's spell arsenal and build support into your plan to protect your primary air push.
If you anticipate an Inferno Tower, consider using Minions or another distraction unit to draw its damage and keep the Lava Hound's path clear.

base.how_it_works: Lightning is a high-damage, area-targeting spell that deals multi-damage and stuns up to three enemy troops or buildings with the highest hitpoints within its radius. It reduces damage to Crown Towers and strikes multiple times if multiple targets are present.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Lightning to finish off low-health Crown Towers or to obliterate support units that can cripple your push. Defensively, it is highly effective against high-value units like the Wizard, Electro Dragon, and Archer Queen, especially when positioned near the enemy tower for extra chip damage. Always ensure there are at least three sufficient targets within the radius to maximize its damage and stun value.
base.counters: Spells like Earthquake or Lightning's own high cost make it vulnerable to strategic counter-spells, and building synergies can mitigate its impact by drawing attention away from core threats. Troops with highly resistant mechanics, such as the Goblin Cage, can absorb multiple strikes, allowing core units to survive its burst damage.
base.combos: Pairing Lightning with Battle Ram or Zappies allows you to quickly eliminate the remaining health of the Crown Tower after the spell has severely crippled nearby threats. Furthermore, using it near a support unit, like the Wizard, ensures the spell can hit multiple targets, maximizing its 3-target stun and multi-damage potential.
tips: Be mindful of the target prioritization: Lightning always targets the three units with the highest hitpoints, so placing a high-HP unit next to a low-HP unit can prevent the low-HP unit from being struck.
When attempting to damage the Crown Tower, aim for the corner of the tower while it's within range to avoid having high-HP defensive units in its radius that will be prioritized by the spell.
Understand that due to its high Elixir cost, using Lightning on only one or two targets often results in a negative Elixir trade, making target density crucial for efficiency.

base.how_it_works: Little Prince is a ground-based Champion troop that deploys as a 3 Elixir card. While stationary, he gains Hit Speed when firing, making him a consistent threat to the tower.
base.champion_ability: By spending an additional 3 Elixir to activate 'Royal Rescue', the Little Prince summons Guardienne, a Knight-stat tank. Guardienne charges in front of the Little Prince, dealing damage, and pushing opposing ground troops away by a short distance. The ability has a 30-second cooldown after its duration ends.
base.ability_timing: It is best activated when a large tank or threat is actively engaging your tower or troops, as the knockback can completely interrupt major attack patterns. Using it too early wastes the cooldown and fails to counter a specific threat, severely diminishing its defensive value.
base.how_to_use: The Prince serves as both a persistent, high-value defensive unit and a strategic attacker. Because of Guardienne's tanking capability, the deck should heavily rely on the combination to neutralize high-threat enemies, making both offensive and defensive plays viable.
base.counters: Because of the knockback effect, units like the Bowler are exceptionally good at countering the duo, as they can hit both Little Prince and Guardienne. Strong spells like Fireball are also threats, as while not enough alone, they can defeat the Prince if leveled higher than him or paired with additional damage.
base.combos: The combination of Little Prince and Guardienne is devastating for controlling large tanks (e.g., Golem, Mega Knight) by interrupting their critical attacks. Using the knockback to push enemy Spear Goblins out of range can also protect the Little Prince from instant death.
base.tips: Always aim to use the knockback effect strategically to reset the attack ramp-up of major threats, which saves crucial chip damage to your towers. Be mindful that while Guardienne tanks well, the death damage from spells like the death bomb can still pose a significant threat to the Little Prince afterward. Remember that the ability costs 6 Elixir total, meaning you are spending a substantial portion of your elixir reserves for its effect.

base.how_it_works: Lumberjack is a melee ground troop that spawns with high health and high damage. Upon being defeated, it triggers a 'Rage' spell, which boosts the damage and attack speed of other units on the field with no additional Elixir cost.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy Lumberjack at the front of a push so that when it dies, the resulting Rage affects surviving, high-damage units. Defensively, positioning it near your Crown Tower ensures the Rage spell boosts the Tower's attack speed, increasing defensive value. Using it as a 'meatshield' for a main tank allows the Rage to boost the tank's remaining damage output.
base.counters: High DPS swarms like Minion Horde or Skeleton Army can counter it by quickly eliminating the unit, removing its ability to survive and boost the push. Area damage spells such as Arrows or Fireball can clear swarms intended to counter, leaving the Lumberjack vulnerable to the opponent's counterattack. For a positive Elixir trade, using a spell like Rocket or a dedicated fighter such as a Hunter can eliminate the threat efficiently.
base.combos: The LumberLoon combo is potent, using Lumberjack to force the enemy to attack first, ensuring the subsequent Rage boost significantly damages the Balloon. Pairing Lumberjack with a tank (like a Giant or Golem) guarantees that when the Lumberjack falls, the Rage boost significantly empowers the main threat, making the full push difficult to handle. Combining it with a Minion Horde allows the Lumberjack to draw fire, letting the Minions capitalize on the resulting Rage to deal massive tower damage.
base.tips: Do not use Lumberjack in isolation; its value is maximized when it's supporting a more expensive, durable, or high-DPS troop. As a punishment card, use him in the opposite lane to force a high-cost unit deployment, capitalizing on the damage and the potential Rage boost for minimal cost. If relying on the Rage, be aware that high-damage swarms or splash units might kill the Lumberjack, but the opponent might then overcommit to the resulting boosted damage.

evolution.what_changes: When destroyed, the Evolution spawns an invisible Ghost that has unlimited health, cannot be targeted by troops, buildings, or towers, but can be affected by spells and disappears after a short time outside the initial Rage Spell's radius.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Ghost provides an extremely versatile defensive asset that can deal damage and support the clearing of moderate pushes, making it valuable for defense. However, the Evo slot might be better used for an Evolution that offers unique synergy or a more consistent win-closing threat, as the Ghost's invulnerability is tied to its initial Rage radius.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: The general counters still apply, but the Ghost's invulnerability to targeting by troops, buildings, or towers means it requires spell or slowdown effects to neutralize.
tips: When Ghost locks on, log the ghost then play a distraction to disrupt its focus.
Defensively, consider playing the Evolved Lumberjack directly on top of a push to capitalize on the Ghost's ability to clear troops.

base.how_it_works: The Magic Archer is a long-ranged, ground troop that launches an attack that pierces through all enemies in its path. It targets both ground and air units and can hit structures (buildings/towers) from a significant distance, even across the bridge or from the inner side.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy him to pressure the opponent's backline buildings, using his long range to snipe towers or force defensive elixir spending. Defensively, he excels at countering structures in the opponent's territory or defeating swarms of low-HP troops like Goblin Gang. Always consider his position relative to the river and towers to maximize his line-of-sight and damage potential.
base.counters: High-damage spells like Poison or Fireball can neutralize him entirely, making them safe, neutral-to-positive elixir trades if they also hit a tower or other threat. The Barbarian Barrel can punish a lone Magic Archer, potentially forcing him to waste an attack or retreat. For massive immediate damage, cards like Lightning or Rocket are effective, though these are negative elixir trades unless they hit multiple high-priority targets.
base.combos: Pairing the Magic Archer with a tank (like a Giant or Golem) maintains offensive pressure while the Archer locks down enemy backlines and buildings. Against ground pushes, supporting him with area damage spells (like Earthquake) increases his efficiency by keeping the opponent's focus split. Using him with fast, swarm-killing units (like Hog Rider) allows him to safely pick off expensive structures the opponent relies on.
base.tips: When pushing, positioning him deep behind your King Tower makes it harder for opponents to reach and target him with spells without triggering your defensive structures. Be mindful of the 'staggered' positioning of enemy buildings; placing him one tile closer to the center of the bridge can often ensure he hits both the building and the Crown Tower. Utilize his ability to hit multiple targets in a line to effectively chip down resources even if the primary push is stalled.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Magic Archer has the same stats as the base card.
hero.hero_ability: With a 2 Elixir ability cost, the player activates Triple Threat to summon a decoy while darting back, and the unit fires a triple shot on its next attack.
hero.ability_timing: This ability is best used when the Magic Archer needs burst damage or distraction, such as when facing a heavy defensive push. Activating it prematurely or when damage is not immediately needed wastes the potential defensive value of the ability.
hero.hero_slot_value: Its high utility in applying burst damage and drawing defensive fire makes it excellent in the Wild slot. However, dedicating a slot to it might be suboptimal if the deck needs reliable direct pressure or needs a dedicated frontline unit.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The counters for the base card still apply to the Hero version.
tips: Use the decoy element of the ability to pull attention away from your main win condition.
Position the Magic Archer to maximize the triple shot damage potential against clustered enemy troops.

base.how_it_works: The Mega Knight is a ground-targeting, melee troop with high hitpoints. Upon deployment, it deals initial area damage to surrounding enemy units and knockback. If positioned between 3.5 and 5 tiles of enemy ground units, it will automatically charge a jumping attack that deals double damage and causes knockback.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use him to absorb initial pressure and initiate large pushes by maximizing the value of his spawn and jump damage on clusters of opponent units. Defensively, his primary role is obliterating support troops and low-HP glass cannons, making him excellent at disrupting clustered micro-units. Always position him so that his jumping attack targets enemies in a straight line to maximize control and continuous damage output.
base.counters: Strong anti-air units, such as air swarms like Minions or Bats, are highly effective because the Mega Knight cannot target or deal damage to air units. Furthermore, high-damage, area-of-effect spells (such as Ice or Lightning) can eliminate him before he reaches peak combat potential, mitigating his threat.
base.combos: Pairing him with the Inferno Dragon creates a synergy where the Mega Knight deals with ranged glass cannons and swarms, while the Inferno Dragon can target and sear the heavy tanks the Mega Knight struggles against. Including supporting ranged troops (like Archers or Wizard) ensures air coverage, as these units provide continuous support the Mega Knight lacks.
tips: When using his jump attack on clustered units, 'kite' them by maneuvering to keep them in a straight line to maximize the directional knockback and follow-up damage.
Do not underestimate his potential against Elite Barbarians; using his jump damage specifically on a pair denies extra hits and allows you to dictate the fight's momentum.
Since he is helpless against air, always ensure you have a cheap spell or an air-targeting ground troop available to cover his critical weakness.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolved Mega Knight gains an uppercut ability on every attack that launches the targeted troop back 4 tiles toward the nearest enemy Crown Tower. This aggressive pushback ensures that targeted troops remain within range for Mega Knight's follow-up jumping attack.
evolution.evo_slot_value: This Evolution creates a major defensive threat by reliably disrupting opponent pushes and keeping large tanks in range for follow-up damage, greatly enhancing its utility against powerful win conditions. However, since its pushback is only on the single targeted unit, relying on this Evolution when facing massive, dispersed swarm troops might be a less efficient use of the slot.
evolution.how_to_use: The Evolved Mega Knight can be used to defeat clustered units by maximizing the value of its spawn damage, and the resulting uppercut-jump combo can be used to rapidly defeat single tanks by repeatedly launching them toward the enemy Crown Tower.
evolution.counters: It remains helpless against air troops, and while its uppercuts deal area damage, only the single targeted troop receives the pushback, allowing heavy ground swarms to keep it from jumping.
tips: Use the Evolved Mega Knight to force large tanks (like Golem or P.E.K.K.A) off the Crown Tower via its pushback, preventing further tower damage from death effects.
When facing ground swarms, position the Evolved Mega Knight between two enemies to maximize the chance of it getting surrounding units, thus preventing it from jumping too often.

base.how_it_works: Mega Minion is an air-targeting, melee Troop that spawns a single unit with moderate hitpoints and high damage. It is primarily effective at dealing with high-HP, low-swarm count targets and is noted for its resistance to being instantly killed by area damage spells.
base.how_to_use: Its main defensive role is engaging ground pushes, where its high damage helps clear moderate-health units. Offensively, it excels as a support unit, clearing out support troops or dealing significant damage while a tank advances. Always position it behind a tank or structure on ranged-attacker lanes to negate the opponent's range advantage, allowing it to survive longer.
base.counters: Cheap, air-targeting, and ranged troops (like Archers or Dart Goblin) are excellent counters because they can engage the Mega Minion from a distance. Buildings like spawner units can also work by forcing the Mega Minion to commit to the structure. For specialized value, Void is noted as the only spell that can destroy it in minimal hits for a neutral Elixir trade.
base.combos: Pairing it with Lava Hound allows the Mega Minion to defend against units trying to destroy the tank while the Lavas provide the main threat. It also synergizes with Miner, where the Mega Minion defends the push and the Miner is deployed further back to distract the opponent's tower, ensuring the Mega Minion can reach its target.
tips: When facing dedicated ranged attackers (Musketeer, Electro Wizard), use the Mega Minion to meet them at range rather than letting them shoot from maximum distance.
Unlike Minions, its moderate health grants it some resilience to minor spells like Zap or Poison, making it versatile for key defensive moments.
Due to its slow hit speed, do not rely on it to handle large swarms; always complement its deployment with splash damage spells or units.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Mega Minion has the same stats as the base card.
hero.hero_ability: The ability is called Wounding Warp and costs 2 Elixir. Upon activation, the Mega Minion warps to the lowest-Hitpoint enemy, dealing damage upon arrival.
hero.ability_timing: It is strategically optimal to activate Wounding Warp when an immediate, high-priority threat needs to be neutralized. Failing to use the ability can mean wasting the activation cost on a unit that was already eliminated or was not the lowest-Hitpoint target.
hero.hero_slot_value: This version gains significant tactical value when the opponent commits multiple threats in different locations, allowing it to surgically target the weakest link. However, it may not be the best choice if the primary goal is generalized crowd control or sustained area damage.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The counters for the Hero version remain similar to the base card, focusing on cheap, air-targeting, and ranged troops.
tips: Utilize Wounding Warp to correct immediate positioning errors, allowing the unit to bypass an opponent's initial formation and strike deep.
Because the ability targets the lowest-Hitpoint enemy, use it when the opponent clusters several small, low-HP threats simultaneously.

base.how_it_works: The Mighty Miner is a high-hitpoint, ground-targeting, melee troop that specializes in dealing increasing damage over time to a single target. Before using his ability, he acts as a classic, aggressive drill-wielding miner.
base.champion_ability: Using his ability, Explosive Escape, costs an additional 1 Elixir and involves digging underground to the horizontally opposite lane. He leaves a bomb at his original spot, which explodes after a 1-second delay, dealing medium area damage and inflicting knockback. The ability has a 13-second cooldown after its duration ends.
base.ability_timing: The optimal time is when an opponent is committing defensive troops to him, maximizing the blast damage and knockback radius. Activating prematurely might only hit unoccupied space, costing the player a crucial Elixir advantage.
base.how_to_use: Deploying him alone at the bridge is a powerful offensive strategy, forcing the opponent to commit resources. He can also defend split-lane pushes with his lane-switching ability, making him highly versatile.
base.counters: Ranged, center-placed towers like the Musketeer or Wizard are strong counters as they hit him regardless of his position. For swarms, a center-placed Tombstone is effective, forcing him to ignore the attacking units.
base.combos: Sending him alone, activating his ability, and following up immediately with a threat like Graveyard or Goblin Barrel is highly effective. The defending troops used against the initial Miner will be weakened and displaced by the bomb, facilitating a counterpush.
tips: When tanking an opposite-side push with Explosive Escape, deploy the secondary threat (like Graveyard) into the target lane *before* using the ability.
Be wary of using his ability purely as punishment; prepared opponents can counter this, making the initial Elixir advantage difficult to secure.
Understand that the total cost of a cycle (Miner + Ability) is 5 Elixir, which dictates how players assess Elixir trades.

base.how_it_works: Miner is a ground-targeting, melee unit that spawns at a placed location and has high hitpoints and moderate damage. It can be placed anywhere in the arena, giving it great placement versatility.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Miner as a surprise tank to distract high-DPS units or generate chip damage, especially by targeting the inner or center tile of a Crown Tower to distract both towers. Defensively, place Miner to tank the damage from dangerous, single-target troops like Princess or Firecracker, forcing them to attack the ground unit. Consider using it to bait out an opponent's counter-push before deploying a spell or heavier support.
base.counters: Buildings or high-damage splash spells (like Earthquake or spells paired with support) are excellent counters as they can eliminate the unit's low HP or force it out of position. Swarm troops, such as a mass of skeletons, work well because they overwhelm the Miner's single-target focus and chip damage potential. High-piercing or spell-based damage is effective as it bypasses the unit's durability and focuses damage on its relatively low structural HP.
base.combos: Combining Miner with an Electro Wizard is powerful: after the Tower locks onto the Wizard, the Miner can be deployed to tank, forcing the tower to retarget the Miner and allowing the Wizard to survive and deal sustained damage. Using Miner first on a tower to bait out a defensive troop, followed by a spell, ensures the opponent's counter-units are removed while still dealing chip damage to the tower. Miner can be paired with fast, light support troops to capitalize on the distraction, allowing them to attack both lanes simultaneously.
base.tips: When deploying the Miner on a tower, placing it on the inner or center tile is highly effective for baiting and distraction because it forces both towers to target the unit. Always look for 'self-destructive' placements where the Miner is expected to take high initial damage, as this can be unexpected and valuable. Against specific high-threat, high-skill units (like Archer Queen or Little Prince), wait until the opponent cannot guarantee their ability use to deploy the Miner.

base.how_it_works: The Mini P.E.K.K.A. is a single-target, ground-targeting melee troop with moderately high hitpoints and extremely high damage. It moves quickly and attacks slowly, making it excellent for quick, high-burst damage output.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use it to quickly eliminate high-HP single-target threats like the Golem or Royal Giant before they can deal damage. Defensively, deploy it against substantial support troops or tank push support units. Always look for opportunities to use its survivability and burst damage to transition into a powerful counterpush.
base.counters: Swarms of low-HP troops (like skeletons) can efficiently defeat it with minimal losses, making positive Elixir trades. Indirect damage or spells (like Arrows) that target multiple units are effective because it requires a high amount of time to deal damage. Because it is slow to attack, fast-moving support cards can overwhelm it before it can deal maximum damage.
base.combos: Pair it with support units like Skeletons or Ice Spirits to easily survive high-damage hits from tanks, letting it reach the main threat. Against massive pushes, deploy it on the support troops first; eliminating these units allows it to save HP for the much more valuable, high-HP tank behind them. This maximizes the trade and conserves its combat longevity.
base.tips: Do not focus it solely on the main tank; prioritizing the support troops (like Wizards or Bowlers) is key to winning the Elixir trade. Because of its speed, position it slightly ahead of your main troop to engage the enemy push immediately and maintain initiative. Be wary of abilities that can knock it back, as it can be vulnerable when recovering from being pushed away.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Mini P.E.K.K.A has the same core troop stats as the base card.
hero.hero_ability: After a 1-second delay, the player activates 'Breakfast Boost' for an additional 1 Elixir. This ability grants the Mini P.E.K.K.A extra Levels based on accumulated pancake meters (up to 5 Levels) and heals him for 30% of his hitpoints. The ability can only be activated once.
hero.ability_timing: The ideal time to use the ability is after the initial powerful strike, maximizing damage on the counterattack and providing a heal. However, if the opponent anticipates it, using the ability might result in the healing being wasted by an immediate spell.
hero.hero_slot_value: The Hero is valuable for maximizing Elixir trades against high-HP anti-tank cards or taking down a Tower on its own. It may not be the best choice if the primary strategic goal is sustained chip damage rather than burst damage.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The counters remain similar, focusing on targeting the Mini P.E.K.K.A before it can use its powerful self-boost ability.
tips: Consider generating the ability meter using support units like the Royal Chef to safely accumulate meters and maximize Levels.
Manipulating the Hero by using other effects, such as Mirror, can further increase the Levels gained, enhancing the card's total damage and HP output.

base.how_it_works: The Minion Horde costs 5 Elixir and spawns six standard Minions in a hexagon formation. These units are reliable air and ground attackers that deal damage to both troops and towers. Their core mechanic is swarm presence and high, immediate threat.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use it to support high-hitpoint tanks like the Giant or Golem, or to distract air-targeting buildings like the Inferno Tower. Defensively, it is highly effective as a powerful counterpush after surviving initial defensive damage. Always prioritize placing it where its six units can maximize damage and minimize exposure to area spells.
base.counters: Area damage cards are the most reliable counter; specifically, Arrows, Wizard, and Lightning (or other effective splash damage) can destroy the swarm efficiently for a positive Elixir trade. For troops, the Cannoneer is excellent as he can eliminate all units in a single attack, while the Hunter's spread-out bullets provide devastating area coverage.
base.combos: Combining Minion Horde with a Miner is highly synergistic, as even single surviving Minions force the opponent to deal with multiple threats (towers and units). Additionally, following a successful defense, pairing it with a fast push (like a tank) maintains momentum and exploits the opponent's reduced resources.
base.tips: Wait to play Minion Horde until the immediate threat has passed, allowing you to leverage its high surprise factor. Because it is so vulnerable to splash damage, be prepared to defend it with inexpensive spells or units rather than letting it be easily eliminated. Always aim for tactical placement to ensure the Minions target support troops first, saving the bulk of the swarm for the primary tank.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolution grants the Minion Horde a brief period of invincibility after taking its first strike, suggesting a defensive capability improvement over the base version.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The ability to become briefly invincible after taking first damage offers surprising resilience, making it valuable for surprise pushes or defending against targeted bursts of damage. However, dedicating this slot might be less impactful than using it for an Evolution that provides continuous utility or superior damage potential.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: The general counters remain applicable, but the temporary invincibility might allow it to survive otherwise devastating initial hits.
tips: Consider timing the deployment to trigger the invulnerability shield from a minor attack, allowing the Minions to safely re-establish their formation.
Use the defensive invulnerability not just against burst damage, but also to bypass enemy spell timings or initial push setup.

base.how_it_works: Minions deploys three single-target, air-targeting, short-ranged troops that attack in a triangular formation. They are low-hitpoint air units, meaning they are immune to ground unit attacks, but they can bypass the river, allowing them to quickly reach all buildings.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, Minions are best used to support high-HP tanks by distracting counters like Inferno Tower or Inferno Dragon, or to deal damage to buildings and structures that ground units struggle with. Defensively, save them for when the opponent lacks Area of Effect (AoE) anti-air units, making them viable for a counterpush. If Minions survive a defense, pairing them with a tank can create a very threatening counterattack.
base.counters: Spells like Arrows are effective for a neutral Elixir trade, taking out the swarm in multiple hits. Royal Delivery can counter them for a neutral trade while also providing an extra unit for defense. Powerful AoE ground units or other air spells are needed to remove them quickly, as their low hitpoints make them susceptible to multiple single-target hits.
base.combos: Pairing Minions with a reliable tanker, such as a Miner, is highly effective, as the Miner's direct path to the tower denies opponent assistance while the Minions keep attention focused. Using Minions alongside a Freeze spell forces the opponent to deal with them for a period, which is highly disruptive and punishing if they only had single-use spells prepared.
base.tips: Do not rely on Minions for consistent counterpushes due to their low hitpoints and vulnerability to targeted spells; conserve them purely for defensive or distraction utility. Be mindful of the opponent's spell cycle and consider when a single Minion survival, rather than the whole swarm, can force a defensive reaction. Because they bypass the river, use them to force opponent building placements into awkward defensive positions.

base.how_it_works: Mirror is a spell that copies the last card played by the player, repeating its effect but increasing its level by one. It does not have a defined Elixir cost and can only be used once per game. The copied card will appear on the field with its enhanced level.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Mirror on high-impact, high-value cards like Goblin Barrel or Elixir Collector to maximize burst damage or resource generation. Defensively, it is excellent for cloning spells that deal immediate damage (e.g., Lightning, Earthquake) or for boosting shields and durability on core troops. Always use Mirror on a card whose counter the opponent only possesses once, maximizing resource denial.
base.counters: Area damage spells, such as Wizard or Executioner, are highly effective because they can counter the enhanced level of the mirrored card regardless of its type. Opponents can also counter by bringing a second, entirely different threat or by utilizing a different card that specifically counters the original card's function. Finally, having another Mirror in hand allows the opponent to repeat the effect, neutralizing the level boost advantage.
base.combos: Combining Mirror with Goblin Barrel forces the opponent to decide if they can counter the greatly enhanced damage output, especially against structures like the King Tower. Pairing it with Elixir Collector is crucial for rapid resource acceleration, allowing the player to deploy follow-up threats or repeat the spell more quickly. Using it with high-synergy spells (e.g., Poison) maximizes area saturation damage, ensuring the enhanced damage penetrates deeply.
tips: Use Mirror on key defensive units with shields (e.g., Dark Prince or Royal Recruits) to maximize their defensive survivability, allowing them to survive damage that would normally kill them.
If the opponent consistently uses one specific spell to counter your main threat (e.g., The Log vs. Goblin Barrel), Mirroring that initial card leaves them resource-starved of that specific counter.
Be aware that the enhanced level also boosts minor units, making them surprisingly durable against spells and single-hit damage, which can completely change key micro-interactions.

base.how_it_works: The Monk is a Champion ground troop that deploys with high hitpoints and moderate damage. He utilizes a unique three-hit combo: the first two attacks deal normal damage, while the final third strike deals extra damage and a guaranteed knockback, regardless of the target's immunity.
base.champion_ability: The Champion ability, Pensive Protection, costs 1 Elixir and activates after a 1-second delay. It grants the Monk 80% damage reduction against all incoming damage and reflects all projectile-based ranged attacks back to the source. The ability also makes the Monk impervious to all forms of knockback and Tornado pull for its duration, with a 17-second cooldown.
base.ability_timing: The optimal time to activate is proactively, using it as a preemptive shield for key units or structures. Activating too late, such as against a large splash spell, minimizes the benefits, as the ability is specifically weak against melee and non-projectile attacks.
base.how_to_use: The Monk is versatile, acting as both a strong offensive threat and a defensive bulwark. Offensively, his powerful third-hit knockback is highly effective, while defensively, his combination of massive damage reduction and projectile reflection makes him an excellent shield.
base.counters: High-damage area control spells like Poison can still counter flanking ranged units near him. Against structures like X-Bow or Mortar, coordinated pressure or multiple reliable damage sources can overwhelm his sustained defenses.
base.combos: Pairing him with cheap ranged units like Firecracker ensures continuous damage and synergy, while deploying him near an Elixir Collector or Crown Tower capitalizes on reflection mechanics to turn enemy spells against the opponent.
base.tips: Do not rely on Pensive Protection against non-projectile attacks, as the damage reduction is significantly lower (65%). Use the threat of reflection, rather than the defense, to force the opponent into unfavorable Elixir trades. Be mindful that anything placed directly in front of the Monk will absorb the full force of reflected projectiles.

base.how_it_works: The Mortar is a ground-targeting building that deploys with high hitpoints and area damage. It has a notable blind spot, preventing it from attacking enemy units located inside its own structure. It deals damage over time using large boulders, but its slow attack speed and limited overall damage output are key weaknesses.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, place the Mortar near the opponent's King Tower to utilize its area damage splash, maximizing damage from units close to the tower. Defensively, position it centrally, closer to your King's Tower, to provide layered resistance that forces the opponent to dedicate resources. Consider using it to eliminate structures, like Elixir Collectors, that are placed two or more tiles away from the enemy King's Tower.
base.counters: Fast units like Hog Rider and Ram Rider are effective because they can reach the Mortar quickly, often making them untargetable due to the Mortar's blind spot. Slow, high-hitpoint building-targeting troops (such as Ice Golem) are excellent counters as they can distract the Mortar entirely using its low damage per second.
base.combos: Utilizing the Tornado can pull enemy troops closer to the tower, greatly enhancing the Mortar's area splash damage and overall burst potential. Pairing it with a tank and a splash unit helps protect the Mortar from units attempting to bypass it and deal consistent damage to the opponent's towers.
tips: To maximize offensive damage, position the Mortar so its area splash damage can reach enemy units that are naturally clustered near the tower.
Be aware that the Mortar's 3.5-second deployment time provides the defender a critical window to place distraction units or set up defensive traps.
If you suspect the opponent's plan is to ignore the Mortar, consider using stuns like Zap or Electro Spirit to force it to retarget, thereby denying potential damage to your King's Tower.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolved Mortar increases its attack speed by 1 second and, critically, spawns a Goblin with every attack. This means that every shot of the Mortar now contributes to the threat level through unit spawning.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The increased attack speed and the consistent spawning of Goblins add significant damage scaling, allowing the Evolved Mortar to deal massive damage, even to the Crown Tower if ignored. However, due to the high damage potential and its capacity to force a spell response, dedicating the Evo slot might be less valuable if the deck already runs another source of late-game tower pressure.
evolution.how_to_use: Generally, deploy it near the opponent's King Tower to maximize the splash damage from the spawned Goblins and the Evolved Mortar's boulders, treating it similarly to the base card's offensive positioning.
evolution.counters: The core counters remain applicable, such as high-health structures or specific defensive synergy. However, the increased Goblin spawn rate makes the Evolved Mortar especially potent and increases the risk associated with simply ignoring the building.
tips: When targeting the Tower Princess, the spawned Goblins are exceptionally dangerous, as the base cards' boulders combine with the Goblins' hits to deal significantly enhanced damage.
Be aware of the King Tower's activation, as the spawned Goblins will take damage from both the King and Crown Towers, which reduces the overall effective damage output compared to the Tower Princess.

base.how_it_works: The Mother Witch is a 4 Elixir Troop that spawns a single-target, air-targeting, medium-ranged ground troop. Upon attacking a troop, she applies a curse that lasts for 5 seconds, and if that cursed unit is destroyed while the curse is active, it spawns a Cursed Hog.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use the Mother Witch to clear out low-health, swarming units, applying the curse to force the opponent to deal with a relentless stream of Cursed Hogs upon their destruction. Defensively, deploy her near a primary tank (like Giant or Golem) to deny fragile counter-push troops, ensuring the opponent also deals with a secondary swarm threat. Use her late game to maximize Cursed Hog spam, preferably when the opponent has committed high elixir into a single push.
base.counters: Mini-tanks and high-damage single-target units (like Knight, Mini P.E.K.K.K., or Prince) are highly effective counters because they deal enough damage to kill the Mother Witch before she can accumulate the curse on multiple targets. Cards with large area damage or splash are good general counters, especially if they are cheap and can eliminate her quickly without needing to charge a target.
base.combos: Pairing her with synergy units like the Electro Giant can boost her effectiveness, as the Electro Giant's reflected damage helps trigger her curse mechanic more reliably. Using her in conjunction with support troops such as the King Tower or an initial tank (e.g., Golem) is excellent, as she pressures the opponent's defenses and forces them to spread their elixir usage, adding a sustained damage threat alongside the primary push.
base.tips: When playing against heavy swarm decks, wait for Mother Witch to engage the Crown Tower first, rather than deploying the swarm immediately, to prevent her from cursing those high-value defensive troops. Remember that the cursed effect does not stack from multiple Mother Witches attacking the same unit, so maintaining single-source damage application is key. While she is good against swarms, she is less effective against high-HP troops that can survive the initial burst of Cursed Hogs.

base.how_it_works: The Musketeer is a single-target, air and ground-targeting unit with a notable long range and high hit speed. It deploys a woman with a musket, allowing it to deal consistent damage to units and structures from a distance.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, maximize her long range by positioning her centrally or slightly in the opposite lane to force opponent reactions. Defensively, place her to hit high-priority threats, using her high hit speed to dismantle single-target threats and swarms of low-HP units. Due to her range, she is safe from several area-of-effect effects, allowing her to repeatedly chip down defensive structures.
base.counters: High-swarm units, such as Minions or Mega Minion, can quickly overwhelm her due to her inability to one-shot them. Large air troops like the Phoenix or Mega Minion are also effective, as they can bypass the initial damage of supporting structures. Additionally, units with focused, heavy damage can force her out of optimal range, making her vulnerable.
base.combos: Pair her with mini-tanks or distraction units to ensure her survival, allowing her to maximize the time she spends dealing damage. When supporting building-targeting troops, her range lets her continuously snipe structures, compensating for the attacker's lower damage or inability to target buildings. She works well with stun or slow spells (like Zap) to buy crucial time against overwhelming enemy pushes.
base.tips:
To maximize damage output, place the Musketeer further from the river. For the most reliable, concentrated damage, position her closer to the river while maintaining range advantage.
Use her range to attack an enemy's Crown Tower while distracting the King's Tower, as the King's Tower may not target her if she is only focusing on the Princess Towers.
Remember that while she is excellent against swarms, she lacks the capacity to one-shot units with high combined HP, requiring backup support for threats like Minion Horde.

evolution.what_changes: After deployment, the Musketeer gains three infinite-range attacks that target the closest troop or building directly in front of her, dealing 1.8 times her normal attack damage. She also loses the ability to target Crown Towers with these sniper shots.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolved Musketeer's unique combination of extended range and specialized sniper shots provides incredible late-game structural and defensive utility. However, dedicating an evo slot might be less impactful if the deck struggles against troops that can force her to waste her sniper shots for minimal value.
evolution.how_to_use: To ensure the full damage potential of her sniper shots, deploy her at a distance from her intended target.
evolution.counters: The Evolved Musketeer is still vulnerable to swarms (like Minions or Goblins) and troops that can force her to waste her sniper shots (such as Goblins or Royal Recruits).
tips: Positioning the Evolved Musketeer allows her to bypass certain buildings and attack structures behind Crown Towers.
She can utilize her sniper shots to destroy objectives like the Bomb Tower, Inferno Tower, or X-Bow, which she otherwise cannot outrange.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Musketeer is a single-target, air-targeting, long-ranged, and ground troop with moderate hitpoints and moderate damage.
hero.hero_ability: For an additional 3 Elixir, the Hero Musketeer activates 'Trusty Turret', which spawns a short-range auto-turret 3 tiles forward of the Musketeer. This turret has a 10-second lifetime and an ability cooldown of 22 seconds after the duration ends.
hero.ability_timing: The ability is best used reactively, such as to distract regular troops or act as a building to attract win conditions. It must be used at a distance to prevent the opponent from luring the Hero Musketeer into the turret's range, which could lead to the Hero Musketeer taking damage.
hero.hero_slot_value: The ability provides significant zone control and defensive utility, making it highly valuable in mid-game skirmishes. However, the reliance on proper range management means its overall value is reduced if the opponent can consistently bait it into danger.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The Hero Musketeer maintains vulnerability to large air troops and focused, heavy damage, similar to the base card.
tips: Use the turret to attract win conditions and force opponent reactions, maximizing the distraction value.
Due to the high combined Elixir cost (4 + 3 = 7), save the ability for critical moments where the turret's zone control is necessary.

base.how_it_works: The Night Witch is a ground-targeting, melee troop that passively summons two Bats every five seconds, with the first wave spawning one second after deployment. When defeated, she spawns an additional single Bat, and her Bats are effective at distracting ground units like the Cannoneer.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy her on high-hitpoint, durable tanks (like Golem or Giant) to maintain pressure while her Bats deal with enemy defenses. Defensively, she is effective against ground armies that the Witch cannot counter, using her initial survivability and damage to assist in counter-pushes. She is also strategically useful when placed near the King's Tower to send a Bat into the opposite lane for light defense support.
base.counters: Air troops (like Miniuns or Phoenix) are highly effective because they ignore her lack of air targeting and can eliminate both her and her fragile Bats. Sparky or Mega Knight can efficiently eliminate her while her Bats cannot distract them effectively enough to survive. Because her Bats are melee-range, any spell or splash damage unit that hits a small area near her will disrupt her ability to safely spawn Bats.
base.combos: Pairing her with a large, building-targeting tank (such as Golem or Giant) allows the tank to soak up damage while the Bats provide crucial support, distracting units like the Inferno Tower. She also synergizes with the Lava Hound, as the Bats distract counter-units to the Hound, allowing the Night Witch to continue advancing toward the tower. Utilizing her with an armored tank allows the tank to deal massive tower damage while the Bats manage threats.
tips: Due to her extreme fragility, always treat her as a setup card that needs protection, not a frontline damage dealer.
Understand that her main value is the sustained, annoying distraction provided by the Bats, which is useful even if the Night Witch herself dies quickly.
When placing her in the back, be aware that the opponent can easily isolate her and eliminate her before the Golem/Giant can effectively use the distraction.

base.how_it_works: P.E.K.K.A is a slow, ground-targeting, melee troop with extremely high hitpoints and massive damage, costing 7 Elixir to deploy. She attacks all enemy units, making her a versatile defensive wall.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy her at the bridge to create immediate pressure and force a large elixir commitment from the opponent. Defensively, wait until a massive ground push is visible, using her high health pool to absorb damage and eliminate multiple high-threat units sequentially. Situationally, deploying her against another high-cost tank can use her to secure a strong counterpush or win a prolonged duel.
base.counters: The Hunter works best when P.E.K.K.A is accompanied by a tank or swarm troops, allowing the Hunter to remain protected while dealing damage. Royal Recruits are an effective counter to a lone P.E.K.K.A because their shields and hitpoints make them difficult to defeat with low-cost spells. Additionally, ranged area damage troops like the Wizard or Bomber can capitalize on her slow speed and lack of inherent anti-air capability.
base.combos: Pairing P.E.K.K.A with the Executioner allows the P.E.K.K.A to handle tank units while the Executioner's area damage controls enemy swarms. The Tornado is excellent support, as it pulls ranged units that P.E.K.K.A struggles to reach, enabling her to finish them off. These combos create balanced defense and offense, maximizing P.E.K.K.A's tanking role.
base.tips: Use P.E.K.K.A proactively on defense to eliminate high-threat units, leaving her with significant health remaining to instantly start a counterattack. Do not commit her alone to the bridge; always ensure supporting cards are ready for the expected immediate response. Be aware of her major vulnerability to unhindered air troops, which can bypass her entirely.

evolution.what_changes: Upon defeating a troop or building, the Evolved P.E.K.K.A spawns a butterfly around its sword, healing 3.5%-15% of its original hitpoints, with the magnitude determined by the defeated unit's hitpoints. This healing can overheal, potentially granting her up to 50% more hitpoints at maximum capacity.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolution provides massive self-healing upon eliminating units, giving it unparalleled longevity and turning it into an extremely powerful defensive wall that can quickly secure a positive Elixir trade. However, dedicating the slot is less valuable if the current deck needs the slot for a different card's unique Hero or Champion ability.
evolution.how_to_use: The Evolved P.E.K.K.A should be deployed aggressively on defense, using its ability to heal from enemy pushes to keep it healthy enough to counterattack.
evolution.counters: The basic counters and vulnerabilities largely remain the same, but the healing mechanism makes it particularly hard to counter with small ground swarms, requiring high DPS units to finish it off quickly.
tips: The healing mechanic is unique: it only triggers if the Evolved P.E.K.K.A deals the finishing blow to a unit, meaning opponents can stall by using high DPS units to keep it occupied without allowing it to heal.
Due to its slow attack rate, use supportive long-range troops (like Musketeer or Dart Goblin) to distract it, preventing it from healing while the main threat is removed.

base.how_it_works: The Phoenix is an air-targeting, melee troop that deals damage and applies knockback upon defeat. Crucially, when defeated, it explodes and spawns a low-HP egg; if this egg is not destroyed within 3.8 seconds, it hatches into a second Phoenix.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use it in conjunction with cards that can reliably support the initial knockback and tank the subsequent damage to keep the unit alive and generating utility. Defensively, deploying it directly on top of a single-target range troop (like Witch or Wizard) can help rapidly chip down its health while bypassing continuous spawns. Always be mindful of timing; the resurrection window requires quick follow-up or defensive protection.
base.counters: Superior anti-air swarms like Minion Horde or large swarms of units (such as Bats) can quickly overwhelm and destroy the Phoenix, often succeeding even when other counters fail. High-damage direct spells (like Arrows) are effective because they can reliably destroy the low-HP egg immediately upon the Phoenix's defeat. Ground-targeting troops that move quickly or charge can kite the Phoenix and target the initial apparition, preventing the egg from being sufficiently protected.
base.combos: Pairing the Phoenix with Clone or Mirror greatly enhances its defensive and offensive utility by generating multiple copies that multiply the death explosion damage and give additional tanking presence. Using it with an Electro Giant allows the Phoenix's high DPS to shred supporting troops, while the Giant tanks the damage and keeps the Phoenix active and regenerating value.
tips: Never underestimate the resurrected Phoenix's ability to support a late-game push, especially when amplified by the Rage mechanic.
Be aware that the egg is not considered a building and is susceptible to damage from spells like Earthquake, which can deal troop damage to it.
If the Phoenix is targeted by Crown Towers, spells like Zap or Giant Snowball can be used strategically to stall for time, buying the necessary seconds for the egg to revive.

base.how_it_works: Poison is an area-damage, air-targeting spell that deals low damage every second for 8 seconds across a medium radius, simultaneously slowing enemy troops by 15%. It is designed to chip away at health over a prolonged period.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, place it on ground units or structures to deal sustained damage and deny swarms, ideally after sending in a non-targeting unit to force the opponent's reaction. Defensively, while slow, its 15% slow can be used in a pinch to manage an opponent's push and buy time for your towers. Given its weakness against high-health, high-damage units, prioritize placing it where it impacts multiple small units or supporting structures.
base.counters: High-health, high-damage troops like Barbarians, Zappies, or Royal Recruits minimize Poison's impact due to the slow, tick-based nature of the damage. Cards that bait Poison, such as Goblin Hut or Ice Wizard, can waste its utility on your core defenses. Furthermore, any large area-clearing spell or a high-burst damage spell can often wipe out its accumulated damage faster than it can tick.
base.combos: It works exceptionally well with the Graveyard to manage enemy counter-pushes and deal consistent damage in a wide area. Pairing it with the Miner allows the prolonged chip damage to occur without interruption, forcing the defender to commit single tanks to manage the threat. Consider using it with other building-damage focused units to eliminate unexpected spawned spawns.
base.tips: Because Poison's damage is not instantaneous, don't rely on it to immediately eliminate swarms; it's better at chipping down their HP while simultaneously slowing them. When deploying, use its slow effect (especially with fast troops) to ensure the target lingers inside the radius for maximum uptime. Be mindful of the timing of its effect; placing it with a slow-spawn unit requires precise deployment to maximize the zone of effect.

base.how_it_works: The Prince is a ground-targeting, melee troop that deploys a single unit. After traveling 2 tiles, it enters a charging state, dealing double damage when it strikes its target. It also possesses the ability to jump over rivers.
base.how_to_use: Use the Prince primarily as a counterattacking unit to quickly stop opponent pushes and threaten the Crown Tower. Deploy him early in offensive pushes to punish opponent Elixir investments, or when the opponent's primary counters are out of rotation. If pushing with a medium-sized force in one lane, place the Prince in the opposite lane to force the opponent to defend both attacks.
base.counters: Strong and durable troops such as P.E.K.K.A., Mega Knight, or Boss Bandit are effective because they can withstand the Prince's charge damage while dealing significant counter-damage. Swarms are dangerous because the Prince can easily be overwhelmed by numerous units, such as those from an Evolved Goblin Giant. Similarly, single, weak glass cannons are not recommended as they will quickly be defeated, wasting Elixir.
base.combos: Pairing the Prince with high hitpoint troops like the Giant or Golem allows the tank to soak damage while the Prince inflicts lethal damage. An even stronger combination is pairing him with the Mega Knight, which provides both splash damage (to clear swarms) and tanking support. The Mega Knight and Prince complement each other by allowing them to easily catch up to each other, preventing separation.
base.tips: To preserve frail supporting units against the charging Prince, it is highly recommended to distract him with a durable tank instead. For defensive purposes, placing the Prince closer to your Tower can maximize the damage dealt to slow, ground-based threats. Use the Miner proactively to draw the opponent's attention away from your Crown Tower, allowing the Prince to connect to the Tower unhindered.

base.how_it_works: The Princess is a long-range, ground troop that spawns an area-damage, air-targeting unit. It deals moderate damage with a slow attack rate and is inherently valuable for its chip damage potential to the opponent's towers.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy her to the bridge to apply consistent, chip damage while forcing the opponent to make reactive decisions. Defensively, place her on the opposite lane to force out a response, saving her position for a delayed counter-push. Be mindful that despite her utility, she can be defeated by many types of spell damage.
base.counters: Area damage spells (like The Log, Arrows, Fireball, or Poison) are the most effective because they cannot be defended against. Additionally, the Miner is a solid counter, capable of two-shotting her and deploying anywhere she is placed. Using cheap troops like Skeletons can distract her at the bridge, forcing a negative Elixir trade for the opponent's reaction.
base.combos: Pairing her with Zap provides a synergistic combination that can instantly clear air swarms like Minion Hordes or Goblin Gangs, keeping her safe to continue damaging the opponent's towers. Her long range makes her an effective siege unit when supported by cheap, protective troops such as Ice Golem or Skeletons, allowing her to safely chip damage from afar.
tips: Use her early at the bridge when she is first deployed to maximize the time the opponent spends reacting to her initial, fast attack.
Position her to safely engage from behind cover to maximize her long-range chip damage while minimizing the risk of being focused down.
Consider deploying her defensively to force the opponent's spell response, even if it means temporarily giving up some tower health, to gain significant Elixir advantage.

base.how_it_works: Rage is a 2 Elixir spell that provides a substantial area buff, increasing the movement speed and attack speed of all allied units and buildings by 30%. This buff is notable for assisting slow troops and accelerating the 'charging' mechanics of certain defenses.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy Rage to support major pushes like those from the Goblin Giant or Mini P.E.K.K.A, especially when the opponent's main counter is unavailable. Defensively, use it on slow-moving, tanking units such as Golems or P.E.K.K.A to help them quickly dismantle enemy threats. Situational timing is key: deploy it when you need rapid engagement, ensuring the entire field is covered to maximize the buff's utility before it fades.
base.counters: Spells like Lightning or Earthquake can counter Rage by removing critical supportive units (e.g., supporting buildings or small swarms) before the buff can take full effect. If the opponent has heavy spell control (e.g., high-damage instant spells), they can negate Rage's value by clearing the threat immediately, making the 2 Elixir expenditure moot.
base.combos: Pairing Rage with Goblin Curse is highly synergistic: Rage boosts the newly spawned Goblins from the Curse, allowing them to overwhelm the enemy tower quickly. Using it with Goblin Barrel allows the enhanced attack speed to accelerate the Goblins' assault on the tower, making it a potent offensive combo.
base.tips: Time Rage so that its buff overlaps with the critical damage phase of an enemy's counter-spell to maximize tempo. Since the buff lasts only up to 2 seconds, focus its use on short-range units or engagements where speed is the single most needed factor. Do not waste Rage on low-priority, easily replaceable threats; save it for the commitment push that dictates the outcome.

base.how_it_works: Ram Rider is a 5 Elixir ground troop that targets buildings, spawning a single-target, melee unit. After traveling 2 tiles, she charges, dealing double damage upon impact. Her accompanying Rider snares enemies, significantly slowing their movement speed by 70%.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use her to kite and chip away at buildings, particularly forcing enemy troops into range for the snaring effect. Defensively, she is excellent at shutting down slow, tanky threats like P.E.K.K.A. or Giant Skeleton by slowing them down, which can then be countered by supporting troops. A good timing tip is to position her near the river to maximize her jumping ability across it.
base.counters: Ranged troops with slowing, stunning, or knockback effects (like Ice Wizard or Electro Wizard) are strong counters because they can keep distance while disabling her. Buildings are effective because they can absorb her attacks and utilize their own ranged power to achieve positive or neutral Elixir trades. Spells with stun or knockback (like Fireball or Zap) provide critical last-resort defense by interrupting her charge.
base.combos: Pairing her with another building-targeting card allows one card to bait structures while the other attacks freely without resistance. Using her alongside the Balloon is synergistic, as the Ram Rider can also attack air troops that are deployed to counter the Balloon. Additionally, passive buildings that spawn melee troops (like Tombstone) are useful, as their spawn locations help ensure enemy units remain in her range.
tips: When kiting Elite Barbarians, avoid having her jump over the river; instead, maintain range to keep her in action for sustained damage and snaring.
She prioritizes non-snared troops over snared ones, meaning a high-threat unit that is slowed might be ignored for a low-health, non-snared target.
Due to her relatively short sight range, do not deploy her with the specific intention of targeting Elixir Collectors placed near the King's Tower.

base.how_it_works: The Rascals deploys a trio of units: a single, melee, ground-targeting 'Boy' with high hitpoints, and two medium-ranged, air-targeting 'Girls' (slingshot users) with lower hitpoints. The Girls deal more damage per second than the Boy, and their ability to split lanes helps with defending.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy the Rascals to focus down a single tower, capitalizing on the combined damage output. Defensively, deploy them in the center of the arena to allow the Girls to split and assist in defending the opposing lane. Always prioritize damaging the Rascal Girls first, as they are the primary damage dealers and can help initiate counter-pushes.
base.counters: Low-cost spells like The Log and Arrows are highly effective because they can immediately destroy the Girls, crippling the Rascals' primary damage threat. Synergy with Goblin Barrel is also strong: the Boy's high HP allows him to tank the spawned Goblins while the Girls continue to attack the tower. The Valkyrie provides a positive Elixir trade by efficiently destroying the vulnerable Girls and then damaging the Boy.
base.combos: Pairing Rascals with buildings or splash damage (like the Wall Breakers or Inferno Tower) forces the opponent to commit spells/troops to the Boy, allowing the ranged Girls to continue uninterrupted damage. Placing Rascals near a structure or behind a temporary distraction unit allows the Girls to remain safe while still contributing damage, maximizing their uptime. Using them against unit spawns like Graveyard is effective, as the girls' dual targeting helps to chip damage to multiple spawned units.
base.tips:
When using the Girls defensively, placing them far from the expected threat path ensures they do not take death damage from siege units. To maximize counterplay, use the Girls to target a low-HP troop on the opponent's side, drawing out defensive spells and commitments.
Be cautious when the opponent has a specific defensive spell (like Poison) which will shred the Girls quickly, negating the Rascals' main damage potential.
Use the Boy as a sacrificial tank at the front to absorb initial damage, ensuring the Girls can operate freely from a slightly safer distance.

base.how_it_works: The Rocket is an area-damage spell that deals high damage with a small radius and inflicts knockback. It targets air and ground units/structures within its blast zone, and deals reduced damage to Crown Towers.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, it excels at finishing off weakened towers, especially aimed at the bottom corner of the Crown Tower for minimal travel time. Defensively, it is highly effective at eliminating medium health troops and structures like the Barbarian Hut or Inferno Tower. Use it to counter high-value setups, such as X-Bow or Tesla+X-Bow combinations, to create a lasting advantage.
base.counters: High-HP, low-deployment-cost tanks (like Golem/Giant) can absorb the initial damage while the Rocket is used on support troops. Low-cost, high-damage swarms of ground units (like Goblin Gang) can overwhelm the single-target focus of the blast. Cards with splash damage that *also* clear the deck (like powerful spell counters) reduce the Rocket's value by eliminating its targets.
base.combos: Pairing it with cheap, low-HP distractors (like Skeletons or Goblins) allows the Rocket to hit the intended target while the support units keep the opponent's attention. Combining it with other defensive spells (like Arrows) allows for greater coverage against multiple medium-health threats. It is excellent when used to eliminate support troops accompanying a threat, maximizing the Elixir gain.
base.tips: When targeting structures, aim for critical weak points (like the bottom corner of the tower) to minimize travel time and maximize damage efficiency. For dealing with medium-health threats, wait until the unit is near a tower or supported by another card to turn a potential negative Elixir trade into a positive one. Always consider the opponent's plan before using a high-cost Rocket on a threat in the back; prioritize counter-pushing high-value decks.

base.how_it_works: Royal Delivery is a 3-Elixir, air-targeting spell that deals moderate area damage and spawns a single Royal Recruit at the point of impact. It is best deployed on the player's side and has a 3-second delay after casting.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, drop it on packed clusters of units or directly onto the opponent's tower for immediate chip damage and the Royal Recruit distraction. Defensively, its high area damage makes it an excellent counter to swarm units or to prevent enemy ground units (like Goblin Barrel) from reaching the tower due to the placement on the Crown Tower. Always be mindful of the 3-second delay when timing a drop defensively.
base.counters: Shielded units, such as Guards, will only have their shields knocked off by Royal Delivery, not eliminated, making it less effective against them. Against powerful single-target spells or high-damage bursts, the spell's damage is moderate and may not be sufficient for a guaranteed elimination. High-piercing, ranged cards can bypass the area splash damage and target individual units more efficiently.
base.combos: Pairing Royal Delivery with Tornado is exceptionally strong, as Tornado clumps enemy units together, maximizing the spell's splash damage and ensuring the spawned Royal Recruit has multiple targets. Using it with Bomb Tower or Goblin Cage buy time for the spell's 3-second deploy delay, while the building simultaneously clumps enemies for the Royal Recruit to finish off.
tips: When defending against a Goblin Barrel, place Royal Delivery directly on the Crown Tower immediately upon seeing the spell deployed, ensuring the splash damage prevents any damage and the Recruit can assist.
To maximize the Royal Recruit's utility, position the spell so the Recruit spawns near the enemy's core push, enabling it to immediately attack a valuable target like a Witch or an opposing tower.

Against ranged glass cannons crossing the bridge, use the spell to hit them right at the crossing point; this helps avoid wasting damage if the opponent targets the tower first, and also applies the push-back effect of the spell.

base.how_it_works: The Royal Ghost is a ground-targeting, melee troop that initially spawns invisible and can only be targeted by area damage or spells. Once it attacks, it becomes visible, and then becomes invisible again if it is not engaging any enemy unit for 1.8 seconds. It hovers, meaning it is targeted as a ground unit, but travels similar to air units.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy him to create surprise damage, ideally supporting a mini-tank or fast attacker like the Hog Rider at the bridge, allowing his speed to dictate the push. Defensively, use him proactively against ranged support troops near the river to take the first strike, saving his health for potential counter-pushes. Always be mindful of his invisibility; it is best used for offensive pressure or to counter enemy charges, rather than reactively tanking for other units.
base.counters: Area damage spells (like Earthquake) or unit attacks that deal area damage (Electro Spirit/Dragon) are highly effective because they bypass his invisibility and do not rely on engaging him first. Heavy, high-HP defensive units (P.E.K.K.A, Three Musketeers) can easily out-sustain his single-target damage output. Similarly, pairing him against enemy charges requires controlling the enemy's engagement, as his speed does not help him physically kite troops.
base.combos: Pairing him with fast attackers such as the Hog Rider or Ram Rider allows his splash damage to efficiently eliminate swarms that threaten the main tank. Using him with mini-tanks like Ice Golem is powerful, but positioning the mini-tank *in front* of him is recommended, as the Ghost's speed will naturally push the mini-tank towards the target. He also has great synergy with quick, low-cost projectiles (like those from ranged units) to eliminate support from a distance.
base.tips: Due to his high speed, he excels at quickly taking out clustered ranged support units; this is a more valuable use than fighting a single high-HP tank. Since he ignores friendly traps and certain invisible units, don't rely on his stealth for flanking maneuvers against other unpredictable battlefield elements. Use his initial invisibility to soak up the opponent's first spell or area damage blast, saving his durability while minimizing the risk of immediate damage.

evolution.what_changes: Upon deployment, the Evolved Royal Ghost summons two Souldiers that remain active after the Royal Ghost retreats, contributing to continuous battlefield presence and area damage. Additionally, the Souldiers' spawn damage provides extreme total damage when attacking while the Royal Ghost is invisible.
evolution.evo_slot_value: This Evolution offers powerful synergy, particularly against predictable or cycle-dependent enemy setups, allowing it to counter multiple waves of units or defend effectively with lingering Souldiers. However, dedicating an evo slot might be less crucial if the deck already possesses other reliable, synergistic counter-push or area-clearing elements.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: It is countered by units that can quickly eliminate clustered troops or spells that can instantly remove the Souldiers, though its overall combat profile remains similar to the base card.
tips: Use the invisible state to maximize the Souldiers' spawn damage by having the Royal Ghost attack, triggering the damage while the King Tower or opponent's troops cannot target it.
Position the Royal Ghost with a tank like the Giant or Golem to allow the Souldiers to protect the tank and maintain continuous pressure after the initial engagement.

base.how_it_works: The Royal Giant is a 6-elixir troop that spawns a slow, single-target, building-targeting, medium-ranged ground unit. Its primary mechanic is that its shots create an area of effect splash damage.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy the Royal Giant to initiate a push on a lane, leveraging its high damage output against towers. Defensively, it can act as a specialized tank if supported by melee troops placed after the Crown Tower has targeted the Royal Giant. A good timing tip is to stage pushes from the back of the King Tower during single elixir time to limit opponent counterplay.
base.counters: High damage swarms (like Skeleton Army or Goblin Gang) are effective because their high output minimizes the damage the Royal Giant can deal. High-HP tanks (such as P.E.K.K.A.) are also good counters, especially if deployed on top of the Royal Giant in its path, forcing it back and reducing damage to the tower.
