Support splash units (like Wizard or Firecracker) are important, as they can defend the swarms that might try to counter the Royal Giant.
base.combos: The Royal Giant synergizes well with the Dark Prince, allowing the Giant to deal damage while the Prince controls swarms and pushes toward the King Tower. It also works well with the Fisherman, who can pull high-DPS troops away from the Royal Giant, forcing the opponent to overspend Elixir. Using support spells and cheap troops ensures the Royal Giant is kept safe while it pressures the towers.
base.tips: When pairing the Royal Giant with ranged support, position the support troops slightly away from the Giant so they can attack enemy units *on top* of the Giant, forcing the opponent to address the full threat. Remember that since the Royal Giant cannot target opposing troops, it benefits significantly from support spells that focus damage on the towers. Be mindful that while the Giant can be an excellent tank, high DPS tanks like P.E.K.K.A. can still destroy the combo if they are well-positioned.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolved Royal Giant maintains its original stats but gains a powerful recoil mechanic: every time it attacks, it deals damage in a 2.5 tile radius and knocks back enemy ground troops by 1 tile.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolved Royal Giant's self-damage and knockback provide constant offensive pressure and control, making it valuable for zone control or preventing critical placements of enemy units. However, since its primary role is ranged, the slot might be better reserved for an Evolution that enhances crowd control or provides a dedicated, mobile threat.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card, though the constant pressure from its recoil makes timing the placement to maximize continuous attack rounds critical.
evolution.counters: The general high-damage counter strategy remains, but the recoil makes swarms less reliable, while immune to knockback units are especially effective.
tips: Use the knockback ability to keep dangerous ground units, such as Mini P.E.K.K.A, slightly away from your tower or to disrupt enemy push lines.
Since the recoil damage forces unit displacement, consider paired support spells that benefit from enemies congregating around the Evolved Royal Giant.

base.how_it_works: The Royal Hogs cost 5 Elixir and spawn four separate, fast, single-target, ground troops that attack buildings. These troops form a horizontal line, are melee fighters, and can jump over the river, making them immune to ground attacks while crossing.
base.how_to_use: Their main offensive role is in a split push, where deploying them slightly off-center allows them to attack both lanes simultaneously. Defensively, they can be positioned to force the opponent to commit large area-damage spells or troops. A good timing tip is to split the push to reduce the amount of splash value the opponent receives from a single attack.
base.counters: Area damage spells are the most effective counters, as they can quickly eliminate the swarm, though large swarms and buildings are also useful. Placing counter-units across both lanes is difficult due to the split nature, so focusing a single area spell is preferred. Cards like the Royal Delivery or high-damage spells like Rocket are also effective as they deal heavy damage.
base.combos: Pairing them with Skeleton Dragons allows the hogs to tank for the support units while the dragons protect them from incoming attacking troops. Using the Three Musketeers provides strong, ranged support that can outrange certain buildings and keep pace with the multi-lane push. The combination of Royal Recruits and Royal Hogs can be evenly split across both lanes, making the duo difficult to spell and control.
tips: When splitting, always ensure the hogs have significant ranged support (like Three Musketeers or Flying Machine) to maintain high damage, as low individual damage is a weakness.

Be wary of the opponent using spells like Fireball, which, while dealing minor damage to the tower, allows them to pressure other vulnerable troops (like Flying Machine or Magic Archer) that you may rely on.
The split push is most potent when the opponent's defense is spread thin, forcing them to commit resources to multiple lanes at once.

evolution.what_changes: The Royal Hogs now deploy as flying troops and travel towards the nearest building. They are immune to ground-targeting troops while flying, and upon attacking or taking damage, they fall to the ground, dealing low area damage on impact.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The ability to fly and gain ground-damage effects offers significant strategic flexibility, allowing attackers to bypass ground-based defenses and force reactions. However, dedicating the Evo slot might be less optimal if the deck lacks reliable high-health tanks to sustain the initial push.
evolution.how_to_use: When initiating an attack, utilize a high health tank to draw damage, allowing the Royal Hogs to maintain aerial travel towards the target tower.
evolution.counters: While splash damage remains effective at clearing swarms, the flying nature means they are immune to ground-targeting troops and are susceptible to other types of splash damage.
tips: Attempt to bait out enemy spells first; if the Hogs are hit by a spell, they will fall, making them vulnerable to subsequent ground-targeting troops.
The flying ability can be leveraged to cancel out the journey to the tower if a structure is placed in their path, forcing them to land early.

base.how_it_works: Royal Recruits deploy six single-target, ground-targeting, melee troops in a horizontal formation, each possessing shields in addition to their normal hitpoints. Due to their formation, they can be split into uneven groups across both lanes, making them highly resilient to single-lane pressure.
base.how_to_use: They are primarily a powerful defensive anchor, stopping split-lane pushes by providing high collective health and damage absorption. Use them to stall high-damage, slow-hitting ground units (like P.E.K.K.A) and to distract air troops, buying time for your towers to chip them down. If you predict a strong, single-lane push, deploy them in the center to maximize the number of troops hit by a single area-effect spell.
base.counters: Area damage spells (like Wizard, Firecracker, or Goblin Demolisher) are highly effective because their splash damage easily obliterates the high number of closely grouped troops. Specific ground splash units (such as the Bowler) can be placed in the center to knock off the shields and hit multiple Recruits simultaneously. Cheap, low-impact spells (like The Log) can provide micro-damage over time, while kiting with cheap units prevents the damage dealt to your towers.
base.combos: Luring the entire group to the center using a cheap distraction unit makes the entire formation vulnerable to massive splash damage from a single card, maximizing the unit's defensive efficiency. Pairing them with units that have ranged attacks (like Magic Archer) allows them to safely engage and counter lower-range splash cards (like Valkyrie) without taking damage in return. Combining them with tanks allows you to use the tank to draw the opponent's attention, allowing the Recruits to stall the damage output long enough for supportive spells or troops to reach the threat.
base.tips: Be mindful of the shield mechanic; remember that excess damage dealt to the shield does not affect the unit's actual hitpoints. Due to their formation, they can be split into 4 troops in one lane and 2 in the other, which is a crucial consideration for setting up spell damage. Never leave more than two Recruits unsupported, as their collective damage output against a single tower can be significant.

evolution.what_changes: After their shield is destroyed, the Evolved Royal Recruits gain the ability to charge towards their target after traveling 2 tiles, dealing 2x damage if they hit the target whilst charging. They spawn with identical stats to the base card, but gain this charging mechanic.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolution's powerful defensive nature and ability to pressure both lanes make it highly valuable for maintaining defensive stability and launching counterpushes. However, if the opposing deck already has high-synergy defensive splash mechanics, the slot might be better used for a card that provides more unique area control or pure damage output.
evolution.how_to_use: The Evolved Royal Recruits are best used when on defense to easily defend both lanes, and they can be used to apply a threatening counterpush when their shields are knocked off.
evolution.counters: The primary counters remain area damage or strong ground splash units; however, the charging ability can put opponents in a difficult situation immediately after the shield damage.
tips: Since they cannot attack air troops, try splitting air swarms to weaken them, but only if the air swarms are supported by a ground distraction to ensure the ensuing charge.
Due to their formation, they can be split into uneven groups (like 4 in one lane and 2 in the other) to create favorable targets for opponents while still maintaining defensive cohesion.

base.how_it_works: The Rune Giant is a 4 Elixir troop that spawns a melee, ground unit with high HP. After a delay, she enchants the two closest allied troops within 8.5 tiles, increasing their damage on every third attack.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy her to buff key ranged support units, like Magic Archer or Princess, allowing them to chip massive damage from the opponent's towers. Defensively, use her to buff high-damage, swarming troops like Electro Wizard or Bandit to quickly eliminate threats. Always position her so she can enchant the desired combat units, as she targets the two closest allies.
base.counters: Area-effect spells are highly effective because they bypass her specific damage boost mechanic entirely. High-cost, single-target threats like Mega Knight or P.E.K.K.A. are poor matchups as they rely on massive burst damage, which the buff doesn't enhance. Cards that attack or interact over long distances (e.g., ranged spells) can ignore her proximity requirements.
base.combos: Pairing Rune Giant with Lightning or Rage is extremely powerful, as these spells maximize the damage cycle of the enhanced units. She also works well with cards that place multiple, persistent attackers, such as a strong cycle unit (e.g., Dart Goblin), ensuring all projectiles benefit from the enhanced damage.
base.tips: Do not use her in building-focused decks (like X-Bow) as her buff specifically ignores structures. Due to her reliance on the 'third attack' cycle, prioritize pairing her with fast-hitting, low-cost units (like Lumberjack or Bandit) rather than slow, tanky units (like Golem). Use her defensively to buff support units from a safe distance, maximizing their longevity.

base.how_it_works: The Skeleton Army costs 3 Elixir and spawns a massive swarm of 15 Skeletons in a scatter formation. These Skeletons are used for their sheer numbers and overall high DPS, primarily targeting single-target units and buildings.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use the Skeleton Army to apply immense pressure against single-target enemy troops and buildings, especially when the opponent's cheap spells are on cooldown. Defensively, its numbers make it highly effective for blocking multiple enemy troops or stalling high-damage air units, which can give your towers time to eliminate threats. If you anticipate heavy single-target pushes, deploy it to force the opponent to spend their most powerful spells, potentially setting up a counter-push.
base.counters: Cheap ranged swarms, such as Archers or Spear Goblins, are excellent counters because their range allows them to kill individual Skeletons safely from a distance. Likewise, air-based counters like Minions or Baby Dragon work well, as the ground-based Skeleton Army cannot target air units. High-damage spells (like Poison or Fireball) are effective against the swarm, but this is a negative Elixir trade unless the spells also hit other key enemy troops.
base.combos: Pairing the Skeleton Army with ranged support allows for maximum coverage; for example, using it with the Tower Princess enables it to fight the swarm's damage while the Princess Tower provides counterattack potential. The Witch synergizes by spawning more Skeletons, amplifying the swarm's numbers for overwhelming offensive pressure, although caution is needed to avoid enemy spell retaliation.
tips: Position the Skeleton Army behind your King's Tower or in the center to cover both lanes and deter immediate opponent aggression.
When defending against spells, be aware that its high troop count means some Skeletons might be out of range, allowing the enemy to bypass intended damage.
If you anticipate a specific high-damage ground troop, wait until it spawns on the surface before deploying the Skeleton Army to avoid initial spawn damage.

evolution.what_changes: The card spawns 15 Skeletons with General Gerry at the back. When the Skeletons are defeated, their shadows will spawn, which have unlimited hitpoints and can be affected by spells. General Gerry is a stronger Skeleton with a shield, a longer range, and a faster attack speed. When General Gerry is defeated, all shadows are instantly defeated, but any surviving Skeletons remain.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evo Skeleton Army adds a resilient core through General Gerry and indestructible shadows, providing sustained battlefield control and forcing opponents to focus fire in multiple ways. However, dedicating the evo slot here means foregoing the potential of other specialized Evolution abilities needed to counter unique or specific threats in the current meta.
evolution.how_to_use: The primary objective is to defeat General Gerry first to prevent the shadows from eliminating anything that attacks them. After he is defeated, the remaining living Skeletons can continue to fight.
evolution.counters: The core mechanic remains the same, but the threat is augmented by the un-targetable shadows and the initial ability of General Gerry's shield, requiring specific spells (like Arrows or Barbarian Barrel) for an effective trade.
tips: Focus on taking down General Gerry to ensure the shadows remain alive and protect the Skeletons' formation.
Be aware that while the shadows cannot be targeted, they can be affected by spells, adding a secondary damage vector to the enemy's spell arsenal.

base.how_it_works: This card deploys a fast, single-target, building-targeting, melee, air troop that hits buildings. Upon reaching a building or being destroyed, the barrel drops, dealing area damage, inflicting knockback, and spawning seven Skeletons in a circle.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy it to target buildings to deal consistent chip damage and force defensive responses. Defensively, it can be used to bait out opponent spells and area damage troops, especially when paired with other threat cards. A good timing tip is to wait until the opponent has committed a spell to bait it, maximizing the defensive value of its death damage.
base.counters: Log, Zap, and Barbarian Barrel are excellent counters because they are cheap spells that can easily eliminate the seven spawned Skeletons, ensuring a positive Elixir trade. Electro Spirit and Fire Spirit also provide positive Elixir trades by chaining damage onto all Skeletons. Cards like Clone can also effectively disrupt the follow-up, making it hard to hit the Skeletons.
base.combos: Combining it with Goblin Barrel creates difficult scenarios where the opponent cannot effectively counter both the initial barrel and the subsequent Skeletons with a single spell. Pairing it with Ice Spirit and Zap allows for a heavy, synchronized spell chain that can eliminate various small troops (like Goblins and Minions) and highly annoying defensive units (like Archers).
tips: The barrel's death damage can significantly chip away at the opponent's tower, so almost never ignore it even if it seems harmless.
Be mindful of the small delay when the Skeletons spawn, as timing large spells (like Electro Spirit) correctly maximizes the positive Elixir trade.
Using a unit like Magic Archer requires precise timing—deploying it when the barrel's shadow is close to the tower maximizes the chance of hitting the clumped Skeletons.

evolution.what_changes: The Evolved Skeleton Barrel is significantly stronger, spawning two barrels instead of one, and these barrels drop their Skeletons both when the barrel reaches 75% hitpoints and when the barrel is finally defeated.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The added secondary barrel and the earlier drop mechanic make this Evolution far more dangerous, forcing opponents to commit more resources or risk two separate damage phases. It might be less valuable when the current meta lacks strong anti-air AoE spells, as this card relies heavily on overwhelming damage output.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: General anti-air troops with high hit speed or AoE attacks remain the primary counters; however, the second barrel's delayed damage makes careful timing against both drop points essential.
tips: Due to its significantly increased combined death damage, this barrel can deal more damage than a Rocket, making it ideal for initiating or extending a major push.
When facing clumps of units, deploying this Evolution can let both barrels drop their damage, and the combined death damage can defeat powerful units like Elixir Blobs.

base.how_it_works: The card deploys two air-targeting, short-ranged units that deal area damage and fly in a horizontal formation. They have moderate hitpoints and moderate damage, and are designed to pair up for combined damage output.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use them in conjunction with large tanks like the Golem or Giant to keep them safe while dealing damage. Defensively, they can trade with enemy units, making it a neutral Elixir exchange if carefully placed in staggered formations to avoid focused damage. Use their dual-lane potential by splitting them across the river to force the opponent to address multiple threats.
base.counters: Area damage spells like Wizard or Firecracker are highly effective as they target air and deal area damage. The Tesla can counter them easily by targeting them directly. Units like Ice Golem, when well-placed at the center of the arena, can kit (distract and weaken) the pair.
base.combos: Pairing them with the Royal Hog allows the Hog to tank while the pair provides air support, protecting the Hog from swarms. They can also be paired with Wall Breakers for a cheap dual-lane push, letting the Dragons damage cheap ranged counters to the Wall Breakers. Using them with the Elite Barbarians combo works well, letting the pair counter swarms while the units are split.
tips: Due to their low hitpoints and high damage, they are excellent at baiting spells, making opponents commit Fireball or Poison prematurely.
When defending, always stagger their placement so that they do not take too much damage from a single source, preserving one for a counterattack.
If you have a Crown Tower support, remember that even air-targeting tanks can be countered by simply having the tower unit soak their hits and take them down.

base.how_it_works: The Skeleton King is a ground-targeting troop that initially spawns as a massive Skeleton. While deployed, any friendly or enemy troops that are defeated grant the Skeleton King a 'soul'.
base.champion_ability: The ability, 'Soul Summoning', costs an additional 2 Elixir to activate and spawns a varied number of Skeletons in a 4-tile radius around him after a 1-second delay. The number of Skeletons spawned depends on the collected 'souls'; he starts with 6 if no souls are present, and can summon up to 16 if he has a maximum of 10 souls.
base.ability_timing: The optimal time is during a mid-to-late game push, after several enemy troops have been defeated by your side, allowing him to maximize his soul count for a massive defensive layer. Activating too early, when no enemy units have died, results in a minimal initial deployment of only 6 Skeletons.
base.how_to_use: Deploy him first to establish presence and allow him to naturally tank damage, and then wait until the opponent has committed troops before activating 'Soul Summoning'. He is excellent in both defense (to bolster a collapse) and offense (to support a push), making him highly versatile.
base.counters: High splash damage cards like Valkyrie or Bowler are effective because their large radius destroys the spawned Skeletons while simultaneously dealing damage to the Skeleton King, creating a positive Elixir trade. Single-target units combined with cheap splash, such as Firecracker and Princess, are also effective at achieving a positive trade by focusing on the main unit.
base.combos: He works exceptionally well with Graveyard, allowing the Skeletons from his ability to quickly deal damage to the opponent's defenses after your Graveyard has been cleared. Furthermore, placing him near a swarm unit, such as a Skeleton Army, allows him to accumulate souls and then punish the opponent with a sudden wave of Skeletons.
base.tips: Remember that friendly troops dying also count as souls, so use him deliberately in swarm combat to pump up his soul count before the opponent can react. Since he and his Skeletons are weak to air, always be mindful of anti-air spells or air support troops. Using him to push away buildings, such as the Royal Giant, can be a subtle strategic way to control lane positioning.

base.how_it_works: Skeletons deploy three fast, single-target, ground-targeting, melee troops in a triangle formation. They have very low hitpoints and very low damage, making them excellent for distracting medium to high-hitpoint units.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, they serve as a cheap distraction to draw out or stall high-hitpoint units like the Prince or Inferno Dragon. Defensively, they provide valuable time and damage, especially against building-targeting troops that cannot fight them. Use them to initiate a threat by splitting them behind your King's Tower to force the opponent to spend Elixir to clear them, keeping your options open.
base.counters: High-damage, area-effect ranged units like the Wizard or Bowler are highly effective as they can wipe out the trio with minimal risk. Similarly, spells are potent counters, as clearing them for free or for minimal Elixir cost creates a negative Elixir trade for the opponent. Units like Goblins can be used to eliminate the Skeletons while creating a secondary threat to the King's Tower.
base.combos: Pairing Skeletons with units like Mini P.E.K.K.A. or Knight allows you to surround and counter high-damage single-targeting threats like P.E.K.K.A. or Elite Barbarians. Against single-targeting ranged troops, placing Skeletons on top of the enemy unit helps negate their range advantage. They also enhance small attacks (e.g., with a Miner) by making the overall threat much more impactful.
base.tips: Don't waste spells on Skeletons; their 1 Elixir cost means that spending a spell will almost always be a negative Elixir trade. When countering large, charged-up abilities, use Skeletons as bait to force the enemy to commit or use their abilities, allowing you time to prepare a stronger defense. If anticipating a Goblin Barrel, splitting the Skeletons behind your King's Tower can force the opponent to dedicate more resources than necessary to eliminate them.

evolution.what_changes: Instead of simply deploying a group of Skeletons, the Evolution spawns 3 Skeletons and then continuously summons an additional Evolved Skeleton with every attack, reaching a maximum of 8 Skeletons.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The perpetual spawning mechanic grants immense value on defense against tanks like Giant or Royal Giant, acting like a perpetually stalling unit that demands precise and powerful spell or splash unit answers. However, the value is limited in open scenarios where the opponent can consistently ignore the swarm or bait out the initial burst.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed with careful consideration for placement, especially placing them behind ranged troops to negate their range advantage and allow the Skeletons to multiply.
evolution.counters: The core counters (e.g., weak spells like Zap or Giant Snowball) still apply, though the massive numbers make them highly susceptible to quick, targeted elimination.
tips: Against single-target units like P.E.K.K.A., the overwhelming number of Skeletons ensures continuous stalling, creating a significant positive Elixir trade.
Use the swarm mechanics to bait out opponent spells, saving those spells for other high-value units to maximize the positive Elixir trade potential.

base.how_it_works: Sparky is a ground troop that charges up over time, dealing massive area damage from a medium range, front-facing tesla coil. Its attack can be reset by any stun effect, and it automatically targets ground units or structures within its range.
base.how_to_use:
Use Sparky primarily for counterattacking pushes to force large Elixir trades due to its high area damage. While slow and vulnerable when alone, she excels as a support unit deployed behind a high-hitpoint tank, which can draw opponent attention to protect Sparky.
Be mindful that her 6-Elixir cost places the player at a temporary Elixir deficit, making superior defense critical until she fires.
base.counters: Electro Dragon or Electro Wizard are highly effective because they can permanently stun Sparky, securing a positive Elixir trade by stopping her attack until she is destroyed. Any direct air troop, such as Minions or Bats, counters her because she cannot target or attack air units, allowing them to safely defeat her.
base.combos: Pairing Sparky with Goblin Giant allows the tanking support to absorb damage while the attached units distract air units. Using a Rage spell can capitalize on Sparky's massive damage output, allowing her to fire a deadly shot on the King or Crown Tower when the opponent is expecting a lull.
tips: Because Sparky cannot attack air troops, always anticipate air deployment and ensure your deck has proper anti-air coverage.
Deploy Sparky at a slight distance from accompanying tanks (like Royal Giant) to ensure she can attack opposing threats without pushing the main tank directly to the tower.
Use support splash units (like Bomber or Wizard) to clear low-HP units in front of Sparky, clearing the path for her main area damage blasts.

base.how_it_works: Spear Goblins deploys three very fast, single-target, air-targeting, medium-ranged, ground troops in a triangle formation. These units have low hitpoints and low damage, but they are highly disposable.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, they are excellent for cheap, constant chip damage behind a higher hitpoint tank. Defensively, their range allows them to stall for time against enemy units, forcing the opponent to commit defensive spells. Always be mindful of opponent prediction; do not rely solely on them if the opponent can immediately counter-spell them.
base.counters: Any damaging spell (like Zap, Poison, or Giant Snowball) is highly effective as it deals high, efficient damage to their fragile structure. Valkyrie can manage them by forcing them into a pattern where they take hits one at a time, allowing your tower to eliminate them. Because they are fragile and air-targeting, they struggle against spell-heavy decks that deny their presence.
base.combos: Pairing them with Mini Tanks provides distraction and damage soaking, allowing the Spear Goblins to deal consistent, low-cost damage safely. Using them with a dedicated tank (like a Giant or Golem) maximizes their supportive role, dealing considerable damage to enemy units and buildings while the tank draws primary fire. This synergy allows the team to maintain pressure and make costly trades.
tips: Utilize their range advantage by keeping them centered to allow both your Crown Tower and the enemy tower to engage them simultaneously. This forces the opponent to manage damage from two sources.
Do not overcommit them: remember they are easily disposable and can be used cheaply to block specific enemy charges or spells.
When making counter-pushes, understand that their effectiveness drops sharply if the opponent has instant, cheap spell removal ready.

base.how_it_works: The Spirit Empress is a versatile troop that can be summoned in one of two forms: a high-damage, air-targeting air troop (when 6+ Elixir is available) or a high-damage, ground-targeting melee ground troop (when 6- Elixir is available). Both forms are single-target attackers with high hitpoints, making them challenging to deal with.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use the Air Form for pressure and tower chip damage, capitalizing on its reach and high damage. Defensively, deploy the Ground Form for emergency defense against ground pushes, utilizing its melee combat style and durability. A key timing tip is to use the cheap Ground Form for immediate defense, saving the more expensive Air Form for a calculated counter-push push later in the game.
base.counters: Swarms are excellent counters to the ground form, such as Goblins or Barbarians, because the Spirit Empress only targets one unit at a time. For the air form, swarms like Spear Goblins, Minions, or Bats are effective. Furthermore, a concentrated spell like a Rocket is highly effective as it can One-Shot Kill (OHKO) the Empress in both of her forms, making it a reliable counter.
base.combos: For maximizing damage, the Spirit Empress works well with heavy push cards like the Golem, Giant, or Royal Giant, allowing it to establish initial threats. Use the Air Form for offensive pressure behind a push, making it hard for the opponent to address both air and ground threats simultaneously. Another effective synergy is pairing it with Lava Hound to create a massive, multi-pronged air threat.
tips: Since she can survive combinations of two-Elixir spells like Fireball or Poison, do not rely on them for reliable damage.
Be mindful that the 3-Elixir Ground Form is best reserved for tactical emergency defense rather than offensive plays.
If you anticipate a counter, deploy the cheap Ground Form first, and then save your 6-Elixir elixir advantage for the more potent Air Form when the lane is open.

base.how_it_works: Suspicious Bush is a 2 Elixir, ground troop that initially spawns invisibly and moves towards the nearest building. Upon reaching a target or being destroyed by a spell, it spawns two Bush Goblins, which are low-HP, ground-targeting, melee troops that deal moderate damage.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy it to force the opponent to use cheap spells or small troops, while simultaneously initiating pressure near the enemy tower. Defensively, use it in the opposite lane to bait out enemy small spells, preserving your defensive spells for a more urgent threat. Be mindful that the spawned Bush Goblins will deal significant damage to certain defensive units, making its presence potentially costly.
base.counters: Small spells like The Log, Magic Archer, and Barbarian Barrel are highly effective because they can destroy the Bush before it spawns the Goblins, or deal with the resulting Goblins quickly. The key is using the right counter to ensure the Goblins don't spawn too close to the enemy tower or your own troops. Since the Goblins are ground-targeted, ranged area damage like Firecracker can clean them up after they spawn.
base.combos: Pairing the Bush with Goblin Barrel or Skeleton Barrel is effective for baiting out enemy small spells, allowing you to secure a larger Elixir advantage for a subsequent push. Similarly, using a Miner near the Bush allows the Miner to tank damage from the Goblins while dealing lethal damage to the tower, creating a high-value offensive threat. Using Skeletons directly on the Bush when it reaches the tower provides an Elixir positive trade and helps clear the Goblins.
base.tips: Recognize that placing a structure like Tesla next to a Bush, if the Bush is the only incoming troop, is unreliable because the Bush's invisibility prevents the tower from detecting it. Instead, use the Bush as a distraction on a secondary lane to force an opponent's spell, guaranteeing a better value trade for your actual defensive spell. Against heavy pushes, deploying the Bush slightly away from your main tank allows you to bait out small spells on the Bush, keeping your defensive spells preserved for the high-HP threat.

base.how_it_works: Tesla is a 4 Elixir building that deploys a single-target, medium-ranged, air-targeting structure with high hitpoints. When idle, it retracts underground, becoming immune to all damage except Freeze and Earthquake.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy it to stall key units or absorb damage from building-targeting troops. Defensively, its underground state makes it immune to most spells and troops, forcing the opponent to use Freeze or Earthquake to engage it. Placing it centrally (3 tiles from the bridge) forces building-targeting units to attack it instead of your towers.
base.counters: Lightning and Earthquake are potent counters as they ignore the underground invulnerability, instantly removing the building and opening up the lane. Graveyard can be effective as a backup counter, capitalizing on Tesla's decent hitpoints and fast attack speed.
base.combos: Pairing Tesla with Skeletons or other small, quick units can bait out opponent spells, allowing the Tesla to cycle back to a hidden position for defense. When used with a defensive synergy like those supporting building-targeting troops, the Tesla can pull multiple threats while the support units maintain pressure.
base.tips:
To force long-range units into its range without sacrificing positioning, preemptively deploy low-cost troops like Skeletons to draw the long-range threat first.
If you suspect the opponent has area damage spells, placing the Tesla one tile from the river helps minimize the chance of the Earthquake hitting both your tower and the structure.
Remember that after a Tesla expires, the opponent may feel safe, so be ready with a quick response troop immediately following the structure's removal.

evolution.what_changes: After deployment, the Evolved Tesla emits an expanding electric pulse that deals high damage to enemies in a 6-tile radius and stuns them for 0.5 seconds. The structure also gains the ability to be frozen while underground, preventing it from popping up when enemies are in range.
evolution.evo_slot_value: Its ability to stun and damage swarms makes it an exceptional defensive tool, particularly for setting up combos with other synergy cards. However, if the player's deck already has powerful anti-swarm cards and other reliable Evolutions, this might not be the highest priority.
evolution.how_to_use: The Evolved Tesla can be timed to deploy when incoming opponent cards cross the bridge to maximize damage and utility during critical moments.
evolution.counters: The core counters remain similar, but the stun effect can add significant utility against swarms of troops that were previously only dealt with by damage.
tips: Use the stun effect aggressively against swarms of cards and light units to create immediate windows of opportunity for damage or retreats.
Leverage the fact that the Evolved Tesla cannot be targeted while underground, allowing low-risk plays like directing a Hog Rider straight to the tower.

base.how_it_works: The Log is an area-damage, ground-targeting spell that appears as a long piece of spiked wood, deploying only on your side of the arena. It has moderate damage and applies a key mechanical effect: knockback to any ground troop it hits, which resets their attack animations.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use it on ground-based swarms or groups of troops advancing toward your towers for damage and knockback. Defensively, it is highly effective for clearing low-HP, ground-only swarms (like Barbarians or Skeleton Army) or pushing enemy tanks out of optimal attack range. Situational timing is crucial: deploy it to redirect heavy units out of the safe zone, such as forcing a Golem or Giant away from your key structures.
base.counters: High-level units are often resistant, as The Log cannot defeat troops that are higher leveled than itself. Other spells, such as those that deal splash damage to the King's Tower (though not mentioned in the text), could be problematic. Furthermore, since it is ground-only, air threats (like Minions or Bats) are completely unaffected by its damage or knockback.
base.combos: It pairs excellently with grounded units, as swarms placed on top of The Log will be immediately hit, and in most cases, eliminated. Combining it with a direct-damage structure (like a Cannoneer shot) can fully counter multiple enemy single-target troops, forcing significant Elixir trades. Using it to push enemy tanks out of range allows your own ranged structures, such as an X-Bow, to connect directly with the opponent's towers.
tips: Watch out for the enemy's ability to use the river edge: deploying The Log can be used to push slow-moving units back over the river into the opponent's territory.
If your opponent places a powerful troop, like a Royal Giant, in the extra space behind a tower, The Log can force them into the King's Tower lane, protecting your more valuable tower.
Because it is a predictable spell, be mindful of its blind spots; any air troops or those positioned outside its path are completely safe from its effects.

base.how_it_works: The Three Musketeers costs 9 Elixir and deploys three separate, independent, air-targeting ground troops in a triangle formation. They maintain long range unless enemy troops get close, at which point they switch to single-target, melee ground combat while retaining high damage.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy them across both lanes to create dual threats, making it difficult for the opponent to counter both areas simultaneously. Defensively, they are powerful against high-damage pushes, capable of stopping both ground and air threats due to their range and versatility. A good timing tip is to support the weaker lane when splitting, ensuring both pushes remain equally threatening.
base.counters: Area damage spells like Fireball or Royal Delivery are highly effective because they apply damage to all three troops simultaneously, guaranteeing a positive Elixir trade for the defender. Specific instant spells like Zap or Lightning are excellent because they can instantly neutralize the entire unit before it deals damage or gets into melee range. Additionally, a well-timed Tornado can group the Musketeers, allowing a single, powerful spell hit to negate the entire threat.
base.combos: Pairing them with bait cards like Elixir Collector or Goblin Barrel maximizes their defensive value, forcing the opponent to waste spells on the bait while the Musketeers remain safe. Combining them with Royal Hogs allows the Hogs to act as a durable distraction and bait, potentially drawing multiple spell hits like Lightning, thus saving the Musketeers from the blast.
base.tips: When splitting the Musketeers, utilize your support cards to strengthen the weaker lane's overall push rather than just deploying the Musketeers there. Be mindful that if the opponent anticipates the split, they may save a specific spell (like a Rocket) to counter the entire combination in one go. Because they are vulnerable to instant spells, keeping them slightly back in the arena minimizes the chance of being rushed by immediate, precise counter-spells.

base.how_it_works: The Tombstone is a Building that automatically spawns two Skeletons every 3.5 seconds, continuously providing a distraction force. When the Tombstone is destroyed, it spawns an additional four Skeletons, creating a burst of damage and distraction.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use the Tombstone to draw fire and attention from enemy single-target units, allowing other troops to take stable damage. Defensively, place it against predictable charging units like the Prince or Bandit, causing them to waste their charges on the Skeletons instead of the tower. For situational timing, utilizing its spawn-on-death mechanic allows for strong defensive counter-pokes.
base.counters: High-damage single-target spells like Lightning or Rocket are highly effective because they eliminate the entire unit instantly and provide burst damage that bypasses the initial setup. Furthermore, troops that can one-shot the spawned Skeletons, such as Spear Goblins, negate the Tombstone’s primary damage contribution and make it purely a distraction.
base.combos: Combining Tombstone with slow-attacking, long-ranged units like the Princess or Mortar allows the Skeletons to occupy the target, greatly increasing the chance that the main damage source will be distracted and chipped down. Another strong synergy is using it with a high-HP support like the Cannoneer or Dagger Duchess, which can target the tank while the Skeletons provide crucial supporting chip damage.
tips: When playing against building-targeting troops, consider placing the Tombstone near the center to maximize the pathing of distracted enemies toward the river.
If the opponent commits an aggressive split push, placing the Tombstone slightly higher or staggered can force a predictable path and funnel the attack.
Due to its low Elixir cost, it is effective for soaking up minor damage from self-destruction mechanics (like Wall Breakers) to protect more vital targets.

base.how_it_works: Tornado is an area-damage spell that features a singularity effect, pulling nearby troops toward its center. It deals low, consistent damage over time and its pull strength is influenced by the target's mass, pulling lighter units further than heavier ones.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use it with splash damage to group enemy troops, maximizing the damage of cards like Musketeer or Wizard. Defensively, pull key enemy units away from your tower to prevent them from dealing damage, or clump them into an area where your own defense can eliminate them. Situational use includes pulling single units away from a threat, such as pulling a Giant Skeleton away from your tower to prevent a death bomb hit.
base.counters: Spells like Earthquake, Arrows, or Zap can be effective because they remove clustered units, negating the damage multiplication the Tornado creates. Units with high resistances or unusual movement patterns, such as Inferno Dragon, can resist the pull to a greater degree than standard ground troops.
base.combos: Combining Tornado with splash damage (e.g., Wizard) is highly effective, as it clumps enemies together, leaving them vulnerable to a devastating blow. It is also excellent for pulling single high-damage units into line with your own towers to allow piercing splash cards to hit multiple targets simultaneously.
tips: When pulling, note that the pull strength is greater on lighter, faster units (like Hog Rider) than on massive units (like Golem).
Use the pull ability to force single-target enemy units to target your key defense troops instead of your tower.
The ability to clump units is more useful than the spell's direct, low damage output; save the spell for maximum positional utility.

base.how_it_works: The Valkyrie is a ground troop that attacks by spinning her axe in a 360° radius around her. This mechanic deals area damage to all ground units surrounding her, making her highly effective against swarms and multiple nearby targets.
base.how_to_use: Defensively, Valkyrie is most effective when played reactively, waiting for support units to cross the bridge to deal maximum damage to the entire push. Offensively, she is potent against low-to-medium health ranged support troops like Wizards or Musketeers, but players should try to deploy her close to the enemy unit to minimize self-damage while approaching them. She can also be used to bait the opponent's tank counter, allowing a stronger tank to advance more safely.
base.counters: Anti-air troops are highly effective as Valkyrie is weak to air units. Specific counters for ground swarms include single-target spells or units with high burst damage, as these can eliminate the Valkyrie before she gets a chance to cycle through the entire area. Troops with long melee range that can strike from afar without entering the 360° radius are also efficient.
base.combos: She pairs well with troops that can target air, such as the Wizard or Electro Wizard, to cover her main weakness to air damage. Combining her with Mini P.E.K.K.A offers a balanced push: Valkyrie handles swarms while the Mini P.E.K.K.A provides focused, supportive damage. For a stronger push, pairing her with Elite Barbarians can generate higher combined damage, helping to break through defending structures or units.
tips: When countering large swarms, deploy her slightly off the swarm's path rather than directly on top to minimize damage taken during the initial deployment.
To maintain maximum health for a counterattack, try to engage enemies in a way that prevents the opponent's charge attacks from activating.
Utilize her quick first attack speed by placing her right next to the tower to catch multiple supporting units, such as those from a Goblin Barrel.

evolution.what_changes: In addition to dealing area damage, the Evolved Valkyrie emits a Tornado upon every attack, pulling all surrounding units towards her and dealing damage within a 5.5 tile radius.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolved Valkyrie's powerful pull effect and increased health make it an invaluable defensive wall that can pull large tanks or multiple troops towards the King Tower for high defensive value. However, if the current deck already has dedicated cards for specific tank or swarm countering roles, the evo slot might be better used on a different utility Evolution.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: High-damage air cards, such as Minions or Bats, are optimal counters, as her attacks cannot hit air, and their high DPS can quickly dispose of her high hitpoints.
tips: Utilize the Tornado effect offensively by placing the Valkyrie near the King Tower to pull enemy support units like Magic Archer or Bowler and potentially activate the tower.
Positioning the Evolved Valkyrie strategically can allow her to fully counter cards like Evolved Royal Giant or Goblin Giant, enabling positive Elixir trades.

base.how_it_works: Vines is a 3-elixir spell that targets and stuns the three highest HP troops or buildings within its radius, grounding any air units caught in the spell. The spell has a specific duration, allowing ground units to hit the trapped targets and dealing damage over time.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Vines by placing it on clustered high-HP threats to secure tower damage or clear multiple supports. Defensively, it excels against high-HP, clumped pushes, providing a reliable stall until your primary defensive unit can engage. For situational timing, deploying it to intercept a shield-dependent threat, like Guards, is particularly effective due to its damage ticks.
base.counters: Buildings with high initial HP, such as the Bomb Tower or Inferno Tower, are excellent counters as they are guaranteed to be among the three highest HP targets. Anti-spell ground traps, such as the Tornado spell, can clear the entire spell, completely negating its value. Furthermore, placing it over high-HP, spread-out units minimizes the effectiveness of the targeting mechanic.
base.combos: Pairing Vines with ground-targeting tanks, such as the Giant or Golem, allows the tank to deal consistent damage to previously untargetable air units. Using it with low-anti-air support cards enables the ground unit to deal damage to both air and ground threats simultaneously. The combination also guarantees high value by making multiple clustered targets vulnerable to ground unit splash damage.
base.tips: Always check the general radius size when predicting its placement, as a slightly larger placement can dramatically increase the number of targets and the spell's overall value. Since it targets the three highest HP, placing it specifically on the enemy's defensive clump (e.g., Wizard + Musketeer) will deny them critical support units. As it is effective against shielded units, anticipate these shields and place Vines to ensure its multiple damage ticks hit the shield multiple times.

base.how_it_works: Void is a 3-Elixir, multi-damage area-of-effect spell with a medium radius. It targets all troops and buildings within its area, dealing significantly more damage when there are very few targets present. Its damage is reduced if multiple units are nearby, but it deals more damage to a swarm if the King Tower is included as a target.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Void on single, high-HP enemy troops (like Electro Dragon or Archer Queen) or defensive structures placed away from the King Tower to guarantee maximum value. Defensively, it excels at removing expensive defensive structures (like defensive buildings) or clearing single, isolated threats. A situational use is targeting a single troop placed directly behind the King Tower to eliminate it without risking damage to the tower itself.
base.counters: Against swarms, use distraction cards like Goblins or Skeletons first, as too many targets will drastically reduce Void's damage output. When attacking buildings, placing Void away from swarms of troops ensures maximum structural damage, rather than dividing its damage across multiple units. Against X-Bow, deploying a cheaper distraction card first ensures the X-Bow is preoccupied, allowing Void to focus its damage on the tower.
base.combos: Pair Void with Electro Spirit to maximize synergy: the stun keeps units inside Void's range, while Electro Spirit pre-weakens heavy hitters, allowing Void's damage to finish them off. When countering Goblin Barrel, using Void alongside a specialized card like Dagger Duchess can nearly fully eliminate the threat, allowing only minor damage to the opponent's tower. The Void can also be used with single-target focus to eliminate difficult, isolated units that might otherwise resist standard spells.
tips: Always aim to maximize the differential damage calculation by ensuring only 1 or 4 targets are inside the radius, as this delivers the highest value per Elixir.
When targeting defensive structures, ensure the area is clear of surrounding troops, as any nearby units will drastically reduce the spell's efficiency.
Be cautious when hitting the King Tower with Void and other troops nearby, as this drastically reduces damage to the supporting units, leading to wasted spell output.

base.how_it_works: Wall Breakers are a pair of fast, area-damage, building-targeting, melee ground troops that deploy in a horizontal formation. Upon attacking a target, they drop and detonate an explosive barrel, destroying themselves in the process on impact.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, they are excellent for cheap pressure, capable of forcing enemy responses and threatening tower damage at a low Elixir cost. Defensively, their high movement speed allows them to be effectively kited against similarly fast melee units to gain valuable tempo or even damage the opponent's structures. A situational use is deploying them in the middle of the arena to split their attack and make counter-play difficult.
base.counters: Spells like The Log, Barbarian Barrel, and Arrows are potent because they deal enough damage to destroy Wall Breakers before they can connect to the tower, especially if the opponent lacks immediate support for a tank. Any spell that can hit both units (like Zap or Tornado) is effective as it removes the threat entirely. Using buildings like Goblin Cage can also neutralize them, though this may not always be Elixir efficient.
base.combos: Pairing them with the Miner creates a low-cost, efficient threat because the enemy Tower will often prioritize the small Miner, allowing the Wall Breakers to safely connect to the main target. Combining them with spells like Zap is highly effective because Zap provides both splash damage and a critical stun, greatly increasing their chances of connecting to a target. Furthermore, placing them in conjunction with another troop that only targets buildings allows them to cripple defensive structures meant to block the push.
tips: Deploying them staggered or in the middle of the arena is a superior tactic for dividing their attacks and making single-lane counter-play difficult.
Use them in tandem with cheap stuns or shields, like Ice Spirit or Zap, to guarantee they connect or to force the enemy to spend Elixir on removing the threat.
When kiting defensively, place them slightly away from the bridge to allow the opponent to chase them ineffectively, which provides valuable chip damage or tempo for the defender.

evolution.what_changes: When the Evolved Wall Breakers are defeated, they explode, dealing high damage and spawning a Runner—an area-damage, building-targeting, melee, ground troop—for each Wall Breaker, in addition to dropping dynamite at their target and destroying themselves on impact.
evolution.evo_slot_value: Its incredibly high damage output on Crown Towers and potential synergy with supporting troops makes it a powerful offensive threat that can apply immense pressure. However, if the opponent has strong building or direct tower damage resources, the Evo slot might be better used elsewhere.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: The core countering strategies remain similar, though specific interactions are changed by the addition of the Runner and death damage.
tips: Avoid placing any units directly next to a building the Evolved Wall Breakers are targeting, as this will result in them being instantly destroyed or taking large amounts of unnecessary damage.
Synergize them with troops like the Miner or Skeleton Barrel; the latter can act as spell bait and a tank, allowing the Evolved Wall Breakers to safely connect and deal their full damage.

base.how_it_works: The Witch is a 5-Elixir ground troop that deals area-damage and air-targeting damage. She passively summons four Skeletons around herself every 7 seconds, with the first group appearing one second after deployment. Her area attack targets units near her.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy the Witch far behind high-health tanks like the Golem to maximize time for her Skeletons to spawn and provide sustained support. Defensively, utilize her high health and Skeleton spawns to reliably distract and counter single-target units with slow attack speeds, giving the tower support. If the opponent has spell support, consider placing her near the river to deny easy spell damage to her tower.
base.counters: Area damage units are highly effective, as they quickly eliminate the Witch and all her deployed Skeletons in one hit. Spells like Fireball or Poison (at higher levels) can take her out completely and are generally the easiest counter. Units like the Electro Wizard or Magic Archer can deal significant damage while simultaneously clearing her supporting Skeletons.
base.combos: The Witch synergizes well with the Skeleton Army, helping generate even more skeletons for overwhelming pressure. To maximize synergy, pairing her with other high-survivability, distracting units ensures the opponent must commit multiple resources to dealing with her extended presence. When cloned, the Witch's clone will also spawn cloned Skeletons, massively increasing battlefield chaos.
tips: Due to her slow first Skeleton spawn, use the Witch defensively only when you anticipate a delay in the opponent's attack, allowing her time to stabilize.
If you are worried about spell damage, deploy the Witch in a side lane to split the opponent's spell resources and force them to use damage on a secondary threat.
Remember that the Witch is most vulnerable immediately after deployment; use it reactively to sustain a push rather than proactively to start one.

evolution.what_changes: After the Evolved Witch spawns a Skeleton, the Evolved Witch heals herself upon the Skeleton's defeat, allowing her to reach 24% more maximum hitpoints. This effect is triggered by every Skeleton spawned by the Evolved Witch.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolved Witch provides a strong defensive tool that gains sustain and increased survivability from the opponent's spell-based damage, making her valuable in defensive compositions. However, she might not be the best slot usage if the deck requires a more immediate burst of utility, as her core value is tied to sustained skirmishing.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: While light spells are still effective, the healing mechanic complicates basic removal, as destroying the spawned Skeletons can sustain her health greatly.
tips: Positioning her behind slow-moving tanks (like a Golem or P.E.K.K.A.) allows her to heal while supporting the slow push, as the spawned Skeletons move faster than the tank.
Using her to support high-cost utility cards like Balloon or Lava Hound can bait powerful spell removal (Rocket or Lightning), creating opportunities for follow-up damage.

base.how_it_works: The Wizard is a mid-cost ground troop that deploys in an area-damage manner, targeting both air and ground units with a medium range. Its attacks deal high damage per second and splash damage, capable of one-shotting small swarms or damaging larger units significantly.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy the Wizard far from your tower to maximize its range and allow its splash damage to clear clustered enemy troops, thereby supporting a high-hitpoint tank. Defensively, utilize its range and splash ability to control large, funneling enemy pushes, making it difficult for opponents to stack units efficiently. Situational tip: If you anticipate an immediate counter, ensure a strong, durable troop is placed near the Wizard, allowing them to absorb incoming damage and keep the Wizard active for counter-pushing.
base.counters: High-damage spells like Fireball or Poison are effective, though they are negative Elixir trades unless used on multiple targets. Moderate to high hitpoint, focused units such as the Mini P.E.K.K.A or Knight are excellent because they can burst the Wizard's moderate HP quickly while taking minimal damage in return. The Bandit, placed far away, can use her dash to initiate an attack and deal significant damage while absorbing minimal damage herself.
base.combos: Pairing the Wizard with a durable tank (like the Ice Golem) allows the tank to draw the enemy's primary damage, maximizing the number of enemy troops the Wizard’s splash damage can hit. Similarly, using the Wizard with the Prince can create a powerful synergy where the Prince targets mini-tanks while the Wizard sweeps away enemy swarms, forcing the opponent to deal with multiple threats simultaneously.
base.tips: When predicting a large swarm, place the Wizard at maximum distance to ensure its splash damage has time to recharge and hit the cluster. Be mindful that using the Wizard's area damage on your own tower can unnecessarily damage it if an opponent immediately counter-pushes that same lane. Do not commit your main counter-push units immediately behind the Wizard if the enemy is pressing the tower, as they may take avoidable damage.

evolution.what_changes: Upon deployment, the Evolved Wizard gains a Fire Shield that prevents him from dying in a single attack. When this shield is destroyed, all pushback capable troops within 3 tiles of the Wizard are pushed back by 3 tiles and take high damage.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The Evolved Wizard is excellent for defending against swarms due to his high splash damage and the defensive shield that protects him from instant kills. However, if the deck needs a card with superior anti-air or exceptional defensive utility, dedicating this slot might be a sub-optimal choice.
evolution.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
evolution.counters: The shield makes him resistant to most high-damage spells, making the counters to the base card largely irrelevant until the shield is destroyed.
tips: Small spells, like The Log, Giant Snowball, or Zap, are ideal for safely destroying the shield and taking out the Evolved Wizard.
Consider placing him in the center of a massive troop cluster to maximize the wipe-out potential of the shield's explosion and subsequent splash damage.

hero.what_changes: The Hero Wizard has the same base troop stats as the regular Wizard.
hero.hero_ability: The Hero Wizard's special ability, Fiery Flight, costs 1 Elixir to activate and sends him flying for 5 seconds. While flying, he gains a 50% movement speed increase, and his fireballs also create 3 tile radius tornadoes that deal damage.
hero.ability_timing: Optimal use involves activating Fiery Flight while he is already attacking enemy troops, which prevents wasting the ability's timing. The ability can also be used to dodge ground-targeting units or mitigate damage from cards that decrease attack speed.
hero.hero_slot_value: Its mobility and area denial potential make it highly valuable for creating tempo and controlling opponent pushes. However, the limited slot space means prioritizing it over other powerful deployable cards must be carefully considered.
hero.how_to_use: Deployed the same way as the base card.
hero.counters: The Hero Wizard remains susceptible to high-damage spells and focused burst damage from anti-air/anti-ground units.
tips: Use the ability to pull back major win conditions like Golem, Giant, and Elixir Golem while simultaneously distracting their support units.
Be aware that certain cards can prevent the ability from working, such as those that drag the Wizard to the ground or negate air attacks.

base.how_it_works: The X-Bow is a ground-targeting building that spawns a single, high-HP crossbow and fires at the opponent's towers and troops from a very long range. Its attack pattern targets the nearest threat, allowing it to damage multiple units and structures.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, placing the X-Bow near the river maximizes its range and forces melee enemy troops to advance into your lane, where they can be chipped down by your own King Tower. Defensively, positioning it between your King Towers keeps it protected while enabling it to engage attackers from afar. Always be mindful of the opponent's hand and deploy the X-Bow knowing when it is likely to be targeted.
base.counters: Anti-building troops like other structures are highly effective because the X-Bow is programmed to ignore them, allowing them to focus all damage on the X-Bow. High-HP tanks such as the Giant or Golem are solid counters as their durability allows them to withstand multiple attacks while simultaneously attacking the X-Bow. Spell damage (like Rocket) is a reliable counter, but players must use a distraction card first, as the X-Bow will continue dealing damage to the tower even if the spell lands.
base.combos: Pairing the X-Bow with a ranged support unit (like Musketeer) allows the combination to maintain overwhelming pressure over time, as the units can keep the opponent distracted while the X-Bow targets the tower. For a defensive setup, placing a tank slightly in front of the X-Bow keeps it safe from immediate air or ground pushes, giving you time to deploy other ranged support cards.
tips: When positioning the X-Bow, placing it one tile away from the river maximizes its total attacking range, giving it the most area coverage.
If anticipating a spell, utilize the X-Bow's distraction by positioning it slightly in the center or corner, forcing the opponent to make a choice about which target to hit.
Avoid predictable placements; moving the X-Bow slightly every match can bait out specific opponent cards, allowing you to better read their threat depth.

base.how_it_works: Zap is a 2 Elixir, area-damage spell that hits ground and air targets with a medium radius. Its primary effect is to briefly stun all enemies within its blast zone for 0.5 seconds.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, use Zap to immediately kill small swarms of units, such as Skeleton Army or Bats, due to its instant deployment. Defensively, it is excellent for resetting charging enemy attacks (like Inferno Tower or Prince) or dealing minor damage to prevent a unit from reaching a key target. Always deploy Zap as early as possible when defending against Goblin Barrel to reduce damage to your tower.
base.counters: The best counters are other highly efficient 2-Elixir area spells like Electro-Spirit/Zap (if available), or any direct, high-value damage source that bypasses the stun (e.g., a direct spell or a highly durable single tank). Because its damage is low, high-damage spells like Lightning are overkill, but even a slightly higher-cost alternative can capitalize on Zap's small AOE.
base.combos: Pairing Zap with Poison allows the 0.5-second stun to keep enemy troops within the Poison radius for an extended period, maximizing the continuous damage output. Combining it with tanks like P.E.K.K.A. lets the tank absorb damage while Zap repeatedly resets enemy siege structures (like Inferno Towers), ensuring the tank gets multiple strong attacks.
tips: Use it when the opponent predicts a strong counter-spell (like Lightning) to ensure the stun is maximized, giving your units extra time to react.
If defending a tower, timing Zap on the opponent's siege machine or high-damage troop can force a target switch, allowing your tower to survive the initial heavy hit.
When dealing with Goblin Barrel, save your first Zap to land exactly when the barrel hits the tower to maximize damage mitigation, rather than wasting it on the incoming army.

evolution.what_changes: Once the initial hit is unleashed, the radius remains in the arena, growing 0.5 tiles while continuing to deal damage and stun as the initial hit. This provides a larger, continuously expanding area of effect compared to the base spell.
evolution.evo_slot_value: The increased area and sustained effect make it highly valuable for reliably stunning and damaging multiple troops, aiding key synergy combos like those with Cannoneer or Hog Rider. However, because of its increased radius, strategic placement is crucial, as using it near the King Tower or behind the Crown Tower can lead to unfavorable outcomes.
evolution.how_to_use: The Evolved Zap can be used for strategic offensive pushes and defenses, requiring careful placement to maximize damage mitigation and synergy with specific units.
evolution.counters: The core concept of countering high-value, charged attacks or swarms remains the same, but the sustained radius of the Evolution makes it a more powerful disruptive tool.
tips: Do not use the Evolved Zap near the King Tower or behind the Crown Tower, as its increasing radius can activate the opponent's King Tower, leading to a worse offensive situation.
To maximize damage and counter high-health units, it can be paired with a second small spell, like Fireball, making it a cost-effective alternative to stronger spells like Lightning.

base.how_it_works: Zappies deploy three single-target, air-targeting ground troops in a triangle formation. They possess medium range and low damage, but their core mechanic is stunning a target for 0.5 seconds with each attack, making them effective at slowing down enemy units.
base.how_to_use: Offensively, deploy them to stall or cripple single-target, slow-moving units, allowing your main damage-dealers time to execute. Defensively, place them near your King Tower to utilize the vertical line-up, maximizing the staggered stun timing against advancing enemies. Situationally, placing them on the opposite side of the bridge can force the opponent to commit spell resources or dedicate forces to contain them.
base.counters: Spells like Fireball or Poison are primary counters because their area damage can destroy the Zappies for a neutral Elixir trade. The Mega Knight is highly effective as his burst damage and spawn damage combo can reduce their HP to a critical point for follow-up attacks. Furthermore, the Executioner or Wizard can eliminate them easily due to their low hitpoints and need for focused attacks.
base.combos: Pairing Zappies with Graveyard is potent, as the Zappies can bait out Poison and survive enough attacks to stun the tower, allowing the Skeletons to spawn and damage the enemy tower repeatedly. Utilizing them with other spell-vulnerable troops can bait out enemy spells, ensuring the follow-up units survive the spell targeting the Zappies. They also support tanks by crippling buildings like the Inferno Tower, preventing them from dealing continuous damage.
base.tips: When positioning Zappies, place them behind the Crown Tower rather than on top of a unit to maximize their charging time and ensure sustained stuns. Use them to specifically bait out opponent spells, protecting other valuable, spell-vulnerable assets on your side of the arena. If you anticipate a Sparky threat, wait for the card to cross the bridge and surround the Zappies to keep them permanently stunned until the Sparky dies.
